the victim of a Grand Theft Motor Vehicle. Because he was only detailed to the Detective Unit
for a short period of time, reinstruction is appropriate regarding this instance.

Specification: Failed to interview a complainant when investigating a criminal complaint
(Manual of Rules 4.18 and the Cleveland Division of Police Detective Manual).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: An attempt to contact the suspect/complainant regarding the Cleveland Division of
Police Report should have been made. Reinstruction is appropriate regarding this instance. In
addition, the complaint was filed beyond the six-month time period in which the incident
occurred; the member’s Collective Bargaining Agreement prohibits discipline when a citizen
complaint is filed more than six months after the date of the alleged event.

Specification: Failed to activate his Wearable Camera System (WCS) during an incident
(General Police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The officer did fail to immediately activate his Wearable Camera System during
adversarial contact with the public. Because there were several other Wearable Camera Systems
active and capturing video at the time and his last Wearable Camera System violation occurred
over five years ago, reinstruction is appropriate regarding this instance.

Specification: Sick leave abuse in the First Quarter of 2023 (General Police Order 1.3.06).

Disposition: The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction placing him on
Step-1 of the Sick Leave Abuse Control Program until August 7, 2024.

Rationale: The officer exhibited a pattern of sick time abuse, to wit: during the First Quarter of
2023, he took forty hours of undocumented sick leave, all of which tied into V-Days.
Additionally, the two-month look-back period revealed an additional sixteen hours of
undocumented sick leave, all of which tied into a V-Day.

Specification: Attended a political rally in Akron, Ohio while wearing a Cleveland Division of
Police, Class-A uniform (Rule 18.30 of The Rules of the Civil Service Commission City of
Cleveland) – Participated in a political advertisement that was aired on a radio station; during the
advertisement, the officer endorsed a political candidate while informing listeners that he was the
“Cleveland Police Patrolmen’s Association former President.” The political endorsement was
also sent by text message advertisement (Rule 18.30 of The Rules of the Civil Service
Commission City of Cleveland).

Disposition: The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: In a photograph of a then Presidential Candidate Donald Trump speech, the officer
was clearly visible in his Cleveland Division of Police uniform. While employed by the City, the
officer endorsed a candidate in an election for judge in a radio spot on a radio station. Because
there may have been confusion regarding the permissibility of these actions, I believe
reinstruction is appropriate.

Specifications: Failed to complete quarterly Wearable Camera System (WCS) audits for the
Fourth Quarter of 2021 (General Police Order 4.06.04) – Failed to properly supervise
subordinates (failed to ensure proper Stop Form procedures were followed) (General Police
Order 2.02.05 and Manual of Rules 1.06).
Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.
Rationale: The officer was new to the position of “Acting Lieutenant” and did fail to complete
quarterly Wearable Camera System (WCS) audits for the Fourth Quarter of 2021. Failed to
ensure proper Stop Form procedures were followed – any information that would have been
captured on the Stop Form would have been contained in the incident report. Reinstruction is
appropriate regarding these instances.

Specification: Failed to complete a Stop Form (General Police Order 2.02.05).
Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction. This corrective
action is within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors
(no active discipline, accepted responsibility) outweigh aggravating factors (none).
Rationale: The officer pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The officer was the
primary detective in the stop, detention and arrest of a male and a Stop Form should have been
completed. Reinstruction is appropriate regarding this instance.

Specifications: Failed to call EMS in a timely manner (Manual of Rules 4.14) – Failed to
complete a Stop Form (General Police Order 2.02.05).
Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction. This corrective
action is within the Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no
active discipline, accepted partial responsibility) outweigh aggravating factors (none).
Rationale: Medical attention was delayed; however, the suspect did not suffer any visible or
incapacitating injuries because of the delay and a Stop Form should have been completed.
Reinstruction is appropriate regarding these instances.
Charges: Failed to Request Medical Attention,Failure to Report/Notify

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Failed to call EMS in a timely manner (Manual of Rules 4.14).
Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction. This corrective
action is within the Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no
active discipline) outweigh aggravating factors (none).
Rationale: Medical attention was delayed; however, the suspect did not suffer any visible or
incapacitating injuries as a result of the delay. Reinstruction is appropriate regarding this
instance.

Specification: Inappropriate comments (Manual of Rules 5.09 and 5.11).
Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.
Rationale: The officer pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. Reinstruction is
appropriate regarding this instance.

Specification: Failed to safeguard an arrested person's property (General Police Order 3.03.02).
Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.
Rationale: The officer pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The officer did fail to
properly secure the prisoner’s property when he set it on the roof of his patrol car and drove
away. He did search for the property; however, it had received damage from falling off of the
patrol car. Reinstruction is appropriate regarding this instance.

Specifications: Refused a direct order (Manual of Rules 2.02, 2.06, and 2.07) – Yelled at her
direct supervisor (Manual of Rules 5.09) – Attempted to leave before turning in an OH-1 prior to
supervisor approval and failed to complete and turn in a BMV 2255 form for an Operating a
Vehicle While Impaired arrest (Manual of Rules 9.08).

Disposition: The charge of refusing a direct order was dismissed. The officer received a Non-
disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction for the remaining charges.

Rationale: Refusing a direct order charge happened after their shift was over and the officer and
the supervisor were both out of uniform, and outside of the Headquarters building. In this instance, the charge of yelling at a direct supervisor more closely comports with a Group I
Violation (conduct that has a negative impact on the operations or professional image of the
Division or negatively impacts relationships with other officers, agencies or the public), the
charge was amended from a Group II violation to a Group I violation. The officer did yell at a
superior officer and failed to complete and turn in reports prior to reporting off duty.
Reinstruction is appropriate regarding these instances.

Specification: Sick leave abuse in the First Quarter of 2023 (General Police Order 1.3.06).
Disposition: The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction placing him on
Step-1 of the Sick Leave Abuse Control Program until September 1, 2024.
Rationale: The officer exhibited a pattern of sick time abuse, to wit: during the First Quarter of
2023, he took one-hundred hours of sick leave, with ninety of the hours attached to vacation
days. Additionally, forty of the one-hundred hours of Sick Leave taken were undocumented, and
of those forty undocumented hours, thirty hours connected to vacation days and the final ten
hours were on a weekend. Additionally, the two-month look-back period revealed an additional
ninety hours of sick leave, with all ninety hours attached to vacation days. Finally, thirty hours of
the ninety hours taken during the look-back period were undocumented, and all thirty
undocumented hours were tied to a vacation day.

Specifications: Failed to arrest the suspect of a Domestic Violence and Rape despite having
probable cause (General Police Order 5.05.01 and Manual of Rules 4.01) – Failed to provide a
domestic violence victim with a Misdemeanor Complaint Statement and failed to complete a
Danger Assessment - Law Enforcement Form (General Police Order 5.05.01).
Disposition: The charge of failing to arrest the suspect of a Domestic Violence and Rape was
dismissed. The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction for the remaining
charge.
Rationale: While the officer did fail to arrest the suspect while having probable cause to do so,
at the time the officer was inexperienced and he did contact a supervisor for guidance and the
supervisor ultimately steered the officer away from the decision to arrest. The officer did fail to
complete the required DA-LE (Danger Assessment - Law Enforcement) Form. Because this is
his first instance of failing to complete this form, reinstruction is appropriate regarding this
violation.

Specification: Inappropriate and discourteous comments while off-duty (General Police Order
1.1.03).
Disposition: The charge of inappropriate and discourteous comments while off-duty more
closely comports with a Group I Violation (conduct that has a negative impact on the operations
or professional image of the Division or negatively impacts relationships with other officers,
agencies or the public), the charge was amended from a Group II violation to a Group I violation.
The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.
Rationale: The officer was captured on a Berea Police Officer’s body camera making
inappropriate and discourteous comments. Reinstruction is appropriate regarding this instance.

Specification: Failed to detect an error in a report or failed to return it for corrections which
resulted in a stolen vehicle not being entered into LEADS (Manual of Rules 1.09, 4.03, and
9.09).
Disposition: The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.
Rationale: The member failed to check, detect and return a report which resulted in a stolen
vehicle not being entered into LEADS. Reinstruction is appropriate regarding this instance.

Specification: Involved in a motor vehicle accident that was reviewed by the Traffic Safety
Analysis Panel and determined to be preventable (Manual of Rules 4.03, 6.03 and 6.05) – Failed
to place his Wearable Camera System (WCS) into event mode prior to responding to a call for
service (General Police Order 4.06.04).
Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.
Rationale: The officer pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. This is the officer’s first
preventable motor vehicle accident and his first Wearable Camera System violation.
Reinstruction is appropriate regarding these instances.

Specification: Failed to fully investigate suspected criminal activity (Manual of Rules 4.18).
Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.
Rationale: The officer conveyed a victim back to her home and the suspect was present. The
victim advised that the suspect was on probation, and with this information, the officer should
have inquired about the crime report the victim completed to determine if there may be a
probation violation. However, because the officer did recover the victim’s property and ensure
her safety, reinstruction is appropriate regarding this instance.

Specification: Involved in a motor vehicle accident that was reviewed by the Traffic Safety
Analysis Panel and determined to be preventable (Manual of Rules 4.03, 6.03 and 6.05) – Failed
to place his Wearable Camera System (WCS) into event mode immediately after becoming
involved in an accident (General Police Order 4.06.04).
Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.
Rationale: The officer pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. This is the officer’s first
preventable motor vehicle accident and his first Wearable Camera System violation.
Reinstruction is appropriate regarding these instances.

Specification: Diminished the Esteem (discourteous comments) (Manual of Rules 5.01 and
5.09).

Disposition: The charge of discourteous comments more closely comports with a Group I
Violation (conduct that has a negative impact on the operations or professional image of the
Division or negatively impacts relationships with other officers, agencies or the public), the
charge was amended from a Group II violation to a Group I violation. The officer received a
Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The officer did make a discourteous comment regarding the suspect; however, the
utterance was not within earshot of the suspect. Reinstruction is appropriate regarding this
instance.

Specifications: Failed to report an allegation of misconduct when a male told him that he was
punched in the face by an officer (General Police Order 1.07.05) – Failed to get a male medical
attention after he requested EMS (Manual of Rules 4.14 and General Police Order 2.01.03) –
Failed to call EMS in a timely manner for a male (Manual of Rules 4.14) – Failed to report a use
of unnecessary force (General Police Order 1.07.05 and 2.01.05) – Witnessed excessive and
retaliatory force, and failed to intervene (General Police Order 2.01.03).

Disposition: The charge of failing to report an allegation of misconduct more closely comports
with a Group II Violation (conduct that is contrary to the values of the Division, or that interferes
with its mission, operations or professional image, or that involves a demonstrable serious risk to
officer or public safety), the charge was amended from a Group III violation to a Group II
violation. Found “Guilty” of two Group I violations, two Group II violations and one Group III
violation. The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction for the charges of
failing to get a male medical attention and failing to call EMS. Considering the officer’s active
discipline history (none), the officer received a twenty-day suspension for the remaining charges.
This corrective action is within the First Group III violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (multiple violations) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline).

Rationale: While the subject did not suffer any visible or incapacitating injuries, the officer still
failed to get medical attention for the subject after he requested EMS and failed to call for EMS
in a timely manner for a subject possibly requiring medical assistance. Reinstruction is
appropriate regarding these instances. The officer failed to report a use of force that was outside
of policy, as there is no evidence that a supervisor or the Internal Affairs Unit was notified, and
he witnessed misconduct when a Cleveland Police detective assaulted and/or used unnecessary,
not proportional, and unreasonable force on a subject and failed to report it. In addition, the
officer also failed to intervene and inquire about medical attention when he had witnessed this
out of policy use of force. Multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may
escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range.
Charges: Failure to Report/Intervene (UOF),Failed to Request Medical Attention

Hearing Held By: Deputy Chief of Homeland Special Operations
Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: As he approached a complainant, he placed his hand on his firearm (General
Police Order 2.01.02).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The officer unnecessarily approached the complainant, creating the possibility of
escalating the encounter as opposed to using distance to de-escalate the situation. Reinstruction is
appropriate regarding this instance.

Specification: Failed to place his Wearable Camera System (WCS) into event mode prior to
responding to assist members calling for assistance and before making contact with a citizen
(General Police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The officer pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The officer has not had a
WCS violation in the last three years. Reinstruction is appropriate regarding this instance.

Specification: Failed to place his Wearable Camera System (WCS) into event mode prior to
responding to assist members calling for assistance and before making contact with a citizen
(General Police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The officer pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. This is the officers’ first
WCS violation. Reinstruction is appropriate regarding this instance.

Specification: Sick leave abuse in the Second Quarter of 2023 (General Police Order 1.3.06).

Disposition: The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction placing him on
Step-1 of the Sick Leave Abuse Control Program until October 30, 2024.

Rationale: The officer exhibited a pattern of sick time abuse, to wit: during the Second Quarter
of 2023, he took sixty hours of undocumented sick leave, with all sixty of the hours attached to
V-Days. Additionally, the two-month look-back period revealed an additional thirty hours of
undocumented sick leave, with all thirty hours attached to V-Days.

Specification: Failed to investigate suspected criminal activity that came to his attention
(Manual of Rules 4.18).

Disposition: The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The officer’s assignment was to assist a victim back home from the Fifth District
headquarters building. Upon conveying the victim back to her home, the suspect was present.
The victim advised that the suspect was on probation, and with this information the officer
should have inquired about the crime report the victim completed to determine if there may be a
probation violation. However, because the officer did recover the victim’s property and ensure
her safety, reinstruction is appropriate in this instance.

Hearing Held By: Deputy Chief of Homeland Special Operations
Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Sick leave abuse in the Second Quarter of 2023 (General Police Order 1.3.06).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction placing him on
Step-1 of the Sick Leave Abuse Control Program until December 13, 2024.

Rationale: The officer exhibited a pattern of sick time abuse, to wit: during the Second Quarter
of 2023, he used fifty-two hours of sick time directly following scheduled vacation days, and
while documentation was provided for forty hours of the fifty-two hours, he showed a pattern of
sick leave abuse when he took sick time on four occasions following scheduled vacation days.

Hearing Held By: Deputy Chief of Homeland Special Operations
Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Sick leave abuse in the Second Quarter of 2023 (General Police Order 1.3.06).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction placing him on
Step-1 of the Sick Leave Abuse Control Program until December 13, 2024.

Rationale: The officer exhibited a pattern of sick time abuse, to wit: during the Second Quarter
of 2023, he took forty hours of undocumented sick leave, of which twenty-four hours preceded
or followed separate scheduled V-Days.

Hearing Held By: Deputy Chief of Homeland Special Operations
Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Failed to activate his Wearable Camera System (WCS) while conducting an
inventory search of a vehicle (General police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The officer pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The officer did fail to
activate his Wearable Camera System during the inventory of a vehicle to be towed. Because he
has not recently had a Wearable Camera System violation, reinstruction is appropriate regarding
this instance.

Specification: Involved in an off-duty motor vehicle crash while in the City of Cleveland and
failed to notify the Communications Control Section (CCS) (General Police Order 8.1.01 and
Manual of Rules 4.20).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The officer pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The officer did fail to
notify the Communications Control Section that she were involved in an off-duty motor vehicle
crash within the City of Cleveland to ensure a supervisor and patrol car were dispatched to the
scene. Reinstruction is appropriate regarding this instance.
Charges: Preventable Motor Vehicle Accident (MVA),Failure to Report/Notify

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Recorded a conversation between her supervisors and herself with her personal
cellphone (General Police Order 4.06.04 and Manual of Rules 2.02, 2.03 and 2.04).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The member was not recording surreptitiously, and when asked if she was recording
she replied that she was recording. Subsequent to this, no supervisor told her to stop recording.
Reinstruction is appropriate regarding this instance.

Specification: Engaged in discourteous and disrespectful behavior while on-duty (Manual of
Rules 5.09).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The officer did engage in discourteous conduct when she became argumentative with
a superior officer in the workplace and raised her voice while others were able to hear and
became disturbed during their work duties. Because the Equal Employment Opportunity
Commission (EEOC) already addressed this incident, reinstruction is appropriate for this
instance.

Specification: Failed to activate her Wearable Camera System during her encounter with a
complainant (General Police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The officer pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The officer did fail to
activate her Wearable Camera System (WCS) during her encounter with a complainant. Though
she was not sure if she needed to have her WCS activated, this was a negative encounter that had
become adversarial. Because she accepted responsibility and has not had a previous Wearable
Camera System violation, reinstruction is appropriate in this instance.

Specification: Failed to generate a report (Manual of Rules 1.07, 9.05 and 9.08).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The officer pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The officer did fail to
generate a report after a complainant told him that she had been assaulted, reinstruction is
appropriate in this instance.

Specification: Failed to notify radio of a Motor Vehicle Accident (MVA) and did not investigate
the reported MVA that came to his attention after being waved down and physically observing
the scene (General Police Order 8.1.02, Manual of Rules 4.18 and Ohio Crash Report
Procedure Manual-Section (ORC) 5502.11).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The parties of the MVA were not present at the scene and allegedly had gone to a
district headquarters to file an accident report. The officer has not had any previous violations of
this nature, reinstruction is appropriate in this instance.
Charges: Failure to Report/Notify,Lack of Service,Neglect of Duty

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Failed to de-escalate (General Police Order 2.01.02).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction. In addition, the
officer is to be retrained in Crisis Intervention through the Training Section.

Rationale: The officer failed to separate the complainant from the Emergency Medical
Technicians that were causing her distress during her time of crisis. The officer has not had any
previous corrective action, reinstruction is appropriate in this instance.

Specification: Failed to separate a complainant from the Emergency Medical Technicians that
were causing her distress during her time of crisis (General Police Order 2.01.02).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction. In addition, the
officer is to be retrained in Crisis Intervention through the Training Section.

Rationale: The officer failed to separate the complainant from the Emergency Medical
Technicians that were causing her distress during her time of crisis. The officer has not had any
previous corrective action, reinstruction is appropriate in this instance.

Specification: Failed to complete quarterly Captain Wearable Camera System audit reports
(General Police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: The charge more closely comports with a Group I Violation (conduct that has a
negative impact on the operations or professional image of the Division or that negatively
impacts relationships with other officers, agencies or the public), the charge was amended from a
Group II violation to a Group I violation. The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of
Reinstruction. This corrective action is within the Group I violation range of the discipline matrix
when mitigating factors (no active discipline, accepted responsibility) outweigh aggravating
factors (none).

Rationale: The officer accepted responsibility. The officer failed to complete quarterly Captain
Wearable Camera System Audit Reports. The uniqueness of the rank structure that involves the
officer’s position and the Neighborhood Impact Community Engagement (NICE) Unit
contributed to the violation, reinstruction is appropriate for this instance.

Specification: Failed to complete quarterly Lieutenant Wearable Camera System audit reports,
did not complete daily Wearable Camera System (WCS) reviews of officers’ footage, and failed
to properly upload and tag a number of WCS videos (General Police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: The charge more closely comports with a Group I Violation (conduct that has a
negative impact on the operations or professional image of the Division or that negatively
impacts relationships with other officers, agencies or the public), the charge was amended from a
Group II violation to a Group I violation. The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of
Reinstruction. This corrective action is within the Group I violation range of the discipline matrix
when mitigating factors (no active discipline, accepted responsibility) outweigh aggravating
factors (supervisory rank).

Rationale: The officer accepted responsibility. The officer failed to complete the quarterly
Lieutenant Wearable Camera System (WCS) audit reports, and failed to properly upload and tag
a number of WCS videos; however, no evidentiary information was lost, therefore reinstruction
is appropriate for this instance.

Specification: Failed to properly upload and tag a number of Wearable Camera System (WCS)
videos (General Police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction. This corrective
action is within the Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no
active discipline) outweigh aggravating factors (none).

Rationale: The officer failed to properly upload and tag a number of Wearable Camera System
(WCS) videos; reinstruction is appropriate for this instance.

Specification: Failed to properly upload and tag a number of Wearable Camera System (WCS)
videos (General Police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction. This corrective
action is within the Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no
active discipline) outweigh aggravating factors (none).

Rationale: The officer failed to properly upload and tag a number of Wearable Camera System
(WCS) videos; reinstruction is appropriate for this instance.

Specification: Failed to properly upload and tag a number of Wearable Camera System (WCS)
videos (General Police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction. This corrective
action is within the Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no
active discipline, accepted responsibility) outweigh aggravating factors (none).

Rationale: The officer failed to properly upload and tag a number of Wearable Camera System
(WCS) videos; reinstruction is appropriate for this instance.

Specification: Failed to properly upload and tag a number of Wearable Camera System (WCS)
videos and failed to activate his WCS until four minutes and fifty-seven seconds after responding
to an incident (General Police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction. This corrective
action is within the Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no
active discipline, accepted responsibility) outweigh aggravating factors (none).

Rationale: The officer failed to properly upload and tag a number of Wearable Camera System
(WCS) videos, and failed to activate his WCS until four minutes and fifty-seven seconds after
responding to an incident; reinstruction is appropriate for this instance.

Specification: Sick leave abuse in the Second Quarter of 2023 (General Police Order 1.3.06).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction placing him on
Step-1 of the Sick Leave Abuse Control Program until March 1, 2025.

Rationale: The officer pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The officer exhibited a
pattern of sick time abuse, to wit: during the Second Quarter of 2023, he used forty hours of
undocumented sick leave, and during the two-month lookback period, he used an additional eight
hours of undocumented sick leave. All forty-eight hours in the review period were
undocumented and either attached to V-Days or compensatory time.

Specification: While on Step-1 of the Sick Leave Abuse Control Program, failed to provide
medical documentation to the Medical Director prior to being returned to duty (General Police
Order 1.3.06 and 1.3.24).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction extending his
placement on Step-1 of the Sick Leave Abuse Control Program until March 1, 2025.

Rationale: The officer did not always provide documentation while he was on Step-1 of the Sick
Abuse Program, however, he did eventually submit documentation for his sick leave.

Specification: Failed to activate his Wearable Camera System (WCS) while interacting with
persons involved in an accident (General Police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The officer pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The officer did fail to
activate his Wearable Camera System (WCS) during his encounter with a complainant and he
has not had a previous Wearable Camera System violation, reinstruction is appropriate in this
instance.

Specification: Sick leave abuse in the Third Quarter of 2023 (General Police Order 1.3.06).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction placing him on
Step-1 of the Sick Leave Abuse Control Program until March 15, 2025.

Rationale: The officer pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The officer exhibited a
pattern of sick time abuse, to wit: during the Third Quarter of 2023, he used one-hundred-fifty-
two hours of sick leave, of which eighty hours were undocumented – of those eighty hours,
seventy-two hours were either on weekends or attached to V-Days. Additionally, during the two-
month look back period, he used twenty-four hours of undocumented sick leave, all of which
were attached to V-Days.

Specification: Responded to a complainant’s call for service and failed to speak with the
complainant as requested (Manual of Rules 4.03 and 4.06).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The officer did fail to confer with a complainant as required by the assignment,
however, he did verify that there was no loud noise upon his arrival and the assignment was
several hours old, reinstruction is appropriate in this instance.

Specification: Responded to a complainant’s call for service and failed to speak with the
complainant as requested (Manual of Rules 4.03 and 4.06).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The officer did fail to confer with a complainant as required by the assignment,
however, he did verify that there was no loud noise upon his arrival and the assignment was
several hours old, reinstruction is appropriate in this instance.

Specification: Responded to a complainant’s call for service and failed to speak with the
complainant as requested (Manual of Rules 4.03 and 4.06).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The officer did fail to confer with a complainant as required by the assignment,
however, he did verify that there was no loud noise upon his arrival and the assignment was
several hours old, reinstruction is appropriate in this instance.

Specification: Failed to include information provided by a caller to a Computer Aided Dispatch
incident (Communications Control Section Policy and Procedures, Call Taking Procedures
1.1.17).

Disposition: The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The member pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The dispatcher did fail
to include information provided by a caller pertaining to being threatened to a Computer Aided
Dispatch incident, reinstruction is appropriate in this instance.

Specification: Failed to follow proper procedure while conducting an investigatory stop by not
completing a Stop Form (General Police Orders 2.02.01 and 2.02.05).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The officer pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The officer did fail to
complete a “Stop Form” following an investigatory stop, reinstruction is appropriate in this
instance.

Hearing Held By: Deputy Chief of Homeland Special Operations
Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Sick leave abuse in the Fourth Quarter of 2023 (General Police Order 1.3.06).

Disposition: The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction, extending his
placement on Step-1 of the Sick Leave Abuse Control Program until May 23, 2025.

Specification: Failed to report and document a Level-1 Use of Force (General Police Order
2.01.05).

Disposition: This charge more closely comports with a Group I Violation (conduct that has a
negative impact on the operations or professional image of the Division or that negatively
impacts relationships with other officers, agencies or the public), the charge was amended from a
Group II violation to a Group I violation. The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of
Reinstruction. In addition, the officer is to attend retraining with the Training Section regarding
Use of Force Reporting.

Rationale: The officer employed a joint manipulation to handcuff a non-compliant subject and
once the handcuffs were secured, the subject stated that her arm was now broken. The officer did
convey the subject to the hospital and a supervisor was notified of this incident; however, the
officer did not complete a BlueTeam entry for this Level 2 Use of Force. I believe reinstruction
is appropriate in this instance.

Specification: Lack of Service (hung up on a complainant and provided no additional service)
(Bureau of Communications Policy 1.1.17).

Disposition: The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The complainant called 911 for emergency service describing an emergency need for
protection from Domestic Violence. The member discussed what the reason for the call was with
the complainant and determined this was not a police matter and tried to inform the complainant
that her call was not a police matter and to speak with a therapist. The complainant continued to
request police assistance during the call; however, the member hung up on the complainant and
provided no additional service. The member failed to advise the caller that the police were
responding to assist her, which may have alleviated the caller’s anxiety. I believe reinstruction is
appropriate in this instance.

Hearing Held By: Deputy Chief of Homeland Special Operations
Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Underreported a level 1 UOF (General Police Order 2.01.05) – Discourteous in
speech (Manual of Rules: 5.09).

Disposition: The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction. In addition, the
member shall attend retraining with the Training Section in Use of Force Reporting and Public
Interaction.

Rationale: The force used was beyond de-minimus. A Level 1 Use of Force was used to control
the subject’s resistive movement; however, the force used was within policy. Further, stating to
the suspect, “Do you want to act like an adult or a child?” is discourteous. Reinstruction is
appropriate in this instance.

Division or that negatively impacts relationships with other officers, agencies or the public); the
charge was amended from a Group II violation to a Group I violation. The member received a
Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The member did respond to the scene and conducted an investigation into the
incident. With further investigation, he may have uncovered the more appropriate charge of

have ordered a cross Aggravated Menacing report. Because he did respond to the scene and did

instance.

Specification: Unprofessional language (Manual of Rules 5.09).

Disposition: The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.

Rationale: The member did use language outside of policy; however, it was used while
attempting to control a possibly armed, felony suspect during handcuffing. Further, after he
successfully handcuffed the suspect, the member’s demeanor changed to employ the tenants of
Procedural Justice (made unbiased decisions, showed respect and trustworthiness, and gave the
suspect an opportunity to tell his side of the story).

Specifications: Unsafe operation of a patrol car (Manual of Rules 6.03) – Wearable Camera
System (WCS) violation (General Police Order 4.06.04).
Disposition: The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction. In addition, the
member shall attend retraining with the Training Section regarding felony traffic stops.
Rationale: The member failed to activate his Wearable Camera System when conducting a
felony traffic stop. Additionally, he placed his patrol car in front of the subject vehicle during a
felony traffic stop. This unsafe operation of the patrol car placed himself and a probationary
patrol officer in a dangerous position in relation to the suspects. Reinstruction and retraining is
appropriate in this instance.

Specification: Failed to notify the Internal Affairs Unit of alleged officer misconduct (General
Police Order 1.07.05).
Disposition: The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.
Rationale: The member did take proactive steps by initiating an investigation into potential
misconduct which she did eventually forward to the Internal Affairs Unit; however, the Internal
Affairs Unit was not immediately notified of potential criminal misconduct as required. Delays
in notification of misconduct can potentially cause the loss of evidence. Reinstruction is
appropriate in this instance.

Specification: Refused mandatory overtime (Manual of Rules 10.01).
Disposition: The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.
Rationale: The member was mandated to work overtime by a superior officer and he initially
refused. It was learned in the hearing that the member found another member to replace him and
fulfill the staffing mandate.

Specifications: Failed to enforce domestic violence statues (General Police Orders 3.4.16) –
Turned off his Wearable Camera System (WCS) while still active on an incident scene (General
Police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: Found “Not Guilty” of failing to enforce domestic violence statues and the charge
was dismissed. The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction for the remaining
charge. This corrective action is within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix
when mitigating factors (accepted partial responsibility) outweigh aggravating factors
(supervisory rank, active discipline).

Rationale: The officer accepted partial responsibility. The officer lacked the probable cause to
make an arrest and a “Named Suspect” report was completed. However, the officer should have
had his Wearable Camera System activated for the entire time he was on scene. Because this is
the officer’s first Wearable Camera System violation, reinstruction is appropriate regarding this
instance.
Charges: WCS Violation,Lack of Service,Neglect of Duty

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Failed to complete three OH-1 crash reports during her tour of duty (Manual of
Rules 9.08) – Failed to secure her Wearable Camera System (WCS) on her uniform (General
Police Order 4.06.04) – Wearing a Cleveland Division of Police sweatshirt in lieu of her uniform
shirt or other approved outermost garment (General Police Order 1.1.12) – Hair extending
below the lower edge of the shirt collar (General Police Order 1.01.01).

Disposition: The member was found “Not Guilty” of failing to complete OH-1 reports and the
charge was dismissed. The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction for the
remaining charges.

Rationale: The member accepted partial responsibility. There has been issues regarding the
logging and forwarding of OH-1 reports and she has no other instances of missing OH-1 reports.
The member has no active discipline and this is her first violation regarding the Wearable
Camera System, the Uniform and Clothing Regulations and the Grooming Standards, I believe
reinstruction is appropriate in these instances.

Mitigating Factor: finding child care under the particular circumstances discussed above).

Improper Procedure: Wearable Camera System (WCS) violation (failed to place in event mode) — failed to wear city-issued N-95 face mask. Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Sick leave abuse in the Fourth Quarter of 2022 (General Police Order 1.3.06) –
Failed to document relevant comments in a CAD incident (Manual of Rules 4.03).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of two Group I violations. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (12/9/2022: three-day suspension), she received a three-day suspension. This
corrective action is within the Second Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (active discipline) outweigh mitigating factors (none). In addition, the
member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction placing her on Step-1 of the Sick
Leave Abuse Control Program until April 27, 2024.

Rationale: The member failed to document relevant comments and information into the CAD
incident. The member used fifty-two hours of undocumented sick leave during the Fourth
Quarter of 2022. Reinstruction is appropriate regarding this instance.

Specifications: Sick leave abuse (×7) (General Police Order 1.3.06) – Tardy (×18) (Manual of
Rules 10.01) – Failed to attend a Pre-disciplinary Hearing (×2) (Manual of Rules 2.02 and
10.01).

Disposition: The seven sick abuse charges are similar in nature and combined. The eighteen
charges of being tardy are similar in nature and combined. The two charges of failing to attend a
Pre-disciplinary Hearing are similar in nature and combined. Found “Guilty” of two Group I
violations and one Group III violation. Considering the member’s active discipline history
(4/19/2022: three-day suspension, 11/10/2022: seventeen-day suspension, 2/7/2023: six-day
suspension), the member received a twenty-seven-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the Group III violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (multiple
violations, active discipline) outweigh mitigating factors (none). In addition, the member was
placed on Step-2 of the Sick Leave Abuse Control Program until October 23, 2024.

Rationale: The officer accepted partial responsibility. The member, on multiple occasions, failed
to provide documentation while on Step-1 of the Sick Leave Abuse Control Program. The
member was tardy on multiple occasions and on two occasions failed to attend a Pre-disciplinary
Hearing as ordered. Multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate the
disciplinary action to the maximum group range.
Charges: Sick Leave Abuse,Tardiness,Attendance and Overtime Violations

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Failed to place his Wearable Camera System (WCS) onto the outermost garment
during a call for service and arrest (General Police Order 4.06.04) – Failed to verify a warrant
after reading on the Mobile Data Computer about possible interstate extradition (Manual of
Rules 4.18 and 4.17) – Took his Wearable Camera System (WCS) out of event mode and placed
it into buffering mode prior to concluding his assignment (General Police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of three Group I violation. The officer received a Non-disciplinary
Letter of Reinstruction for not having his WCS on his outermost garment. Considering the
officer’s active discipline history (none), the officer received a one-day suspension for the
remaining charges. This corrective action is within the first Group I violation range of the
discipline matrix when aggravating factors (multiple violations) outweigh mitigating factors (no
active discipline).

Rationale: The officer did not have his Wearable Camera System on his outermost garment as
required, reinstruction is appropriate in this instance. He was advised of an arrest warrant and
failed to take the proper steps to execute the warrant; placed his Wearable Camera System into
buffering mode while still on an assignment, and failed to reactivate his Wearable Camera
System. Multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary
action to the maximum group range.

Specifications: Signed into MCAD and used a patrol car while off-duty (Manual of Rules 6.01)
– Disobeyed a direct order (Manual of Rules 2.02 and 2.06) – Left the City limits without
permission (Manual of Rules section 4.07).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation, one Group II violation and one Group III

violation. Considering the member’s active discipline history (none) the member received a Non-
disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction for leaving the City limits without permission and a thirteen-
day suspension for the remaining charges. This corrective action is within the First Group III

Violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (multiple violations arising
from a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group
range, liability created by using a Cleveland Division of Police vehicle while off-duty and
without permission) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline).
Rationale: The member did use a City vehicle while off-duty and without permission to travel
outside of the City of Cleveland, and when ordered to answer to that allegation by Form-1, he
refused to complete the Form-1. Multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may
escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range.

Specifications: Using and possessing marijuana (Ohio Revised Code 2925.11 and Manual of
Rules 2.01 and 5.07) – Call taking violations (×3) (failed to display courtesy and
professionalism) (Communications Control Section 1.1.17 and Manual of Rules 5.08 and 5.09) –
Refused mandatory overtime (Communications Control Section 1.3.07) – Call taking violation
(failed to question caller if EMS was needed) (Communications Control Section 1.1.17).
Disposition: The charge of using and possessing marijuana more closely comports with a Group
II Violation (conduct that is contrary to the values of the Division, or that interferes with its
mission, operations or professional image, or that involves a demonstrable serious risk to officer
or public safety); the charge was amended from a Group III violation to a Group II violation.
Found “Guilty” of five Group I violations and one Group II violation. Considering the member’s
active discipline history (4/29/2024: 6-day suspension, 7/18/2024: Written Reprimand,
10/18/2024: 9-day suspension) the member received a thirteen-day suspension. Because this is
her third sustained Group II violation occurring within a three year period, the discipline
automatically progresses into the Group III violation range. This corrective action is within the
First Group III Violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (multiple
violations, active discipline for discourteous behavior) outweigh mitigating factors
(truthfulness). In addition, the Cleveland Division of Police will require the member to complete
an Employee Assistance Program including at minimum two (2) random drug screen tests during
the next twelve (12) month period and shall attend retraining in courtesy with the Training
Section.
Rationale: During her Garrity interview the member was truthful and admitted that she had used
marijuana while it was prohibited by Ohio Law. This admission came through her truthfulness
during her interview and without any other evidence to support this violation. The member also
repeatedly failed to demonstrate courtesy and respect in her speech while taking calls for service
(a Safety Telephone Operator is the first step in the Cleveland Division of Police helping people
in crisis). In addition, a Safety Telephone Operator needs to gather necessary information so that
a proper response can be made; she failed to do so when she did not inquire if EMS was needed
for a female assault victim with a head wound. She refused overtime seven times during the
Third Quarter of 2024; however, she did make up two mandatory overtime refusals, leaving five
net refusals in the Third Quarter of 2024.
Charges: Drug & Alcohol Testing Policy Violation,Unprofessional Conduct,Refusal of Mandatory Overtime,Lack of Service

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Failed to de-escalate (General Police Order 2.01.02) – Grooming standards
violation (beard) (General Police Order 1.04.01) – Diminished the esteem (Manual of Rules
5.09).
Disposition: The diminishing the esteem charge more closely comports with a Group I Violation
(conduct that has a negative impact on the operations or professional image of the Division or
that negatively impacts relationships with other officers, agencies or the public); the charge was
amended from a Group II violation to a Group I violation. Found “Guilty” of three Group I
violations. Considering the member’s active discipline history (none) the member received a
Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction for the grooming standards violation and a four-day
suspension for the remaining charges. This corrective action is within the Group I violation range
of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (multiple violations arising from a single event
or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range, supervisory rank)
outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline).
Rationale: The member accepted responsibility for having a beard that was not trimmed above
the neckline at all stages and had the appearance to exceed 1⁄4 inch in length, reinstruction is
appropriate for this instance. He did fail to properly de-escalate when he interacted with a
juvenile, almost goading or challenging the juvenile to attack him. After the attack, in the
presence of several workers, he belittled the juvenile several times when he called him a “brat.”
While this behavior is outside of Division expectations, especially for supervisory personnel it
more closely comports with a Group I violation. Multiple violations arising from a single event
or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range.
Charges: Failed to De-escalate,Uniform/Grooming Violation,Conduct Unbecoming / Diminished Esteem

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Arrested for Operating Vehicle Under the Influence of Alcohol or Drugs (OVI)
(Parma Heights Ordinance 333.01 and Manual of Rules 2.01) – Cited for Reasonable Control
(Parma Heights Ordinance 333.025 and Manual of Rules 2.01) – Arrested for Using Weapons
While Intoxicated (Parma Heights Ordinance 672.03 and Manual of Rules 2.01) – Diminished
the esteem of the Division (Manuel of Rules 5.01) – Submitted an incomplete report (Manual of
Rules 9.12).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation, three Group II violations and one Group
III violation. Considering the member’s active discipline history (11/21/2022: 7-day suspension, 9/12/2023: 7-day suspension) the member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction for
submitting an incomplete report and a twenty-day suspension for the remaining charges. This
corrective action is within the First Group III Violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (active discipline, multiple violations arising from a single event or incident
may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range) outweigh mitigating factors
(none).

Rationale: The member was arrested by the Parma Heights Police Department for Driving
Under Influence of Alcohol or Drugs, Reasonable Control (of a motor vehicle) and Using
Weapons While Intoxicated. He entered pleas of “No Contest” and was found “Guilty” in Parma
Heights Mayor’s Court of Driving Under Influence of Alcohol or Drugs, Reasonable Control (of
a motor vehicle) and an amended charge of Attempted Peace Disturbance. While the officers
were conducting their investigation leading to the member’s arrest, he engaged in behavior that
would reasonably tend to diminish the esteem of the Division which included urinating in public
and not immediately complying with Parma Heights police officers when being handcuffed. The
member did request a supervisor to respond to the scene of Incident #2023-268529 (#2023-
268591) to determine the appropriate action to take; he did fail to include key information in a
Criminal Trespass report, to include two suspects that were on scene and that there was possible
video of the incident – reinstruction is appropriate in this instance. Multiple violations arising
from a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range.
Charges: Conduct Unbecoming / Diminished Esteem,Arrest / Criminal Charge,Failure to Report/Notify

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Tardy (×9) (Manual of Rules 10.01) – Was live on Social Media (TikTok) while
working at telephone position #265 in the Bureau of Communications (General Police Order
7.03.03) – Failed to provide documentation while on Step-1 of the Sick Leave Abuse Control
Program (General Police Order 1.3.06) – Refused mandatory overtime (×3) (Bureau of

1.3.07 and Manual of Rules 10.01).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of five Group I violations and one Group III violation. Considering

Reinstruction placing her on Step-2 of the Sick Leave Abuse Control Program until May 12,
2026. This corrective action is within the First Group III Violation range of the discipline matrix
when aggravating factors (active discipline, multiple violations arising from a single event or
incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range) outweigh mitigating
factors (none).

Rationale: The member was tardy on nine occasions, three each in July, September and
December 2024, with times ranging from five minutes late to eighteen minutes late. She violated
General Police Order 1.3.06 when she failed to adhere to the requirements of the Step-1 Sick
Leave Abuse Control Program, to wit: she failed to provide medical documentation for sick
leave taken on October 25, 29 and 30, 2024. During the Fourth Quarter of 2024, she refused
mandatory overtime on three occasions: October 7, 2024, December 15, 2024, and December 28,
2024. Finally, she was live on the social media platform TikTok while working at her telephone
position and taking calls for the Cleveland Division of Police. Multiple violations arising from a
single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range.

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Walked a naked female into the University Hospital Emergency Room without
properly covering her (General Police Order 1.01.03 and Manual of Rules 5.09) – Grooming
standards violation (fingernails longer than one-quarter inch) (General Police Order 1.04.01).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation and one Group II violation. Considering
the member’s active discipline history (none) the member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of
Reinstruction for the grooming standards violation and a six-day suspension for the remaining
charge. This corrective action is within the First Group II violation range of the discipline matrix
when mitigating factors (University Hospital police officers took custody of the female from the
patrol car and walked her into the hospital, no active discipline) outweigh aggravating factors
(none).

Rationale: The member transported a naked female to University Hospital. Once at the hospital,
she had a second opportunity for the female to access a covering for her body, but again she
failed when she did not intervene as she allowed University Hospital police officers to walk the
naked female into the hospital. The member’s fingernails clearly exceed one-quarter inch beyond
her fingertip. Because the member has no prior grooming violations, reinstruction is appropriate
in this instance.

Specifications: Operated a Cleveland Police vehicle that was involved a motor vehicle crash that
was reviewed by the Traffic Safety Analysis Panel (TSAP) and determined to be preventable
(Manual of Rules 4.03, 6.03 and 6.05) – Failed to consider the condition of his patrol car while
engaged in emergency response driving (General Police Order 4.01.01) – Unprofessional
language (Manual of Rules 5.09) – Emergency response driving violation (intermittent siren
usage) (General Police Order 4.01.01) – Derogatory term (Manual of Rules 5.09).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of five Group I violations. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction for the
preventable motor vehicle accident and a three-day suspension for the remaining charges. This
corrective action is within the Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating
factors (multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary
action to the maximum group range) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline).

Rationale: The Traffic Safety Analysis Panel determined that the Motor Vehicle Crash of June
18, 2024, was preventable. The member did continue to drive the patrol car while it was clearly
taking undercarriage damage, resulting in the vehicle becoming inoperable and requiring
extensive repairs. His language was outside of Division expectations and in conflict with Manual
of Rules 5.09, to wit: he stated, “Our car fucking slammed into you,” referred to another as
“homeboy,” and then stated, “That I don’t give a fuck about.” The member did fail to properly
utilize the patrol car’s siren for emergency response driving – only using it intermittently as
opposed to leaving it activated and his language was outside of Division expectations and in
conflict with Manual of Rules 5.09, to wit: he stated, “I’ll tase you, bitch.” Multiple violations
arising from a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum
group range.
Charges: Preventable Motor Vehicle Accident (MVA),Unprofessional Conduct,Offensive Remarks,Travel Violation

Charge & Discipline Decision: Improper Procedure; Lack of Service - WCS Violation.  Patrol Officer Nicholas Ramey received a one (1) workday suspension without pay (Group I  violation, violation of D.N. #18-014. Mitigating Factor: no active discipline) for the WCS  violation. The disciplinary action for the remaining charge was dismissed. However, Patrol  Officer Nicholas Ramey received a non-disciplinary, corrective Letter of Re-Instruction (Group I  violation).

Improper Procedure: failed to investigate – Wearable Camera System (WCS) violation (failed to place in event mode) – failed to provide medical attention. The failing to investigate charge was dismissed. Patrol Officer Kevin Nussbaum received a seven (7) workday suspension without pay for failing to provide medical attention (First Group II violation. Aggravating Factors: active discipline and multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range

– Mitigating Factors: none). In addition, Patrol Officer Kevin Nussbaum received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction for the remaining charge.
Charges: WCS Violation,Lack of Service,Failed to Request Medical Attention

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Arrested for Disorderly Conduct While Intoxicated (Lakewood Ordinance
509.03(b) and Manual of Rules 2.01) – Arrested for Use or Possession of Paraphernalia
(Lakewood Ordinance 513.04 and Manual of Rules 2.01) – Sick leave abuse (×2) (c/w
Lakewood Arrest) (to attend criminal proceedings) (General Police Order 1.3.06) – Failed to
notify the Division of arrest (Manual of Rules 3.10) – Submitted to a reasonable suspicion
alcohol and drug test; the results were positive for cocaine (Manual of Rules 2.01 and 5.07) –
Submitted to a reasonable suspicion alcohol and drug test; the results were positive for THC
(Manual of Rules 5.07) – Diminish the esteem of the Division (Manual of Rules 5.07) – Sick
leave abuse in the Second Quarter of 2024 (General Police Order 1.3.06) – Sick leave abuse in
the Third Quarter of 2024 (General Police Order 1.3.06) – Tardy (×3) (Manual of Rules 10.01).

Disposition: The charge of sick leave abuse in the Third Quarter of 2024 was dismissed. Found
“Guilty” of five Group I violations, two Group II violations and three Group III violations.
Considering the member’s active discipline history (none) the member received a twenty-day
suspension. In addition, the member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction placing
her on Step-1 of the Sick Leave Abuse Control Program until April 21, 2026. This corrective
action is within the First Group III Violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating
factors (multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary
action to the maximum group range) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline).

Rationale: The charge of sick leave abuse in the Third Quarter of 2024 was dismissed as it is
duplicative of using sick leave in connection with the Lakewood arrest and using sick leave to
attend a criminal proceeding. The member was arrested by the Lakewood Police Department for Disorderly Conduct While Intoxicated and Use or Possession of Paraphernalia. While the
member was in jail and while attending court proceedings for the above crimes, she
inappropriately used sick leave in lieu of unpaid administrative leave and did not make the
required notification to the Cleveland Division of Police of her arrest and criminal court
proceedings. The member submitted to a reasonable suspicion alcohol and drug test, and the
results were positive for cocaine and THC. During her Garrity interview, she admitted to
consuming one line of cocaine and using marijuana after her arrest and before returning to work.
The member acted in a manner that would tend to diminish the esteem of the Division when she
told the Lakewood Police Department that she worked for the police while wearing clothing with
the Cleveland Division of Police badge during her arrest and while in Possession of
Paraphernalia. The member exhibited a pattern of sick time abuse, to wit: during the Second
Quarter of 2024, she used 77 hours of undocumented sick leave and was tardy three times within
a 30-day period. Multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate the
disciplinary action to the maximum group range.
Charges: Sick Leave Abuse,Tardiness,Conduct Unbecoming / Diminished Esteem,Arrest / Criminal Charge,Drug & Alcohol Testing Policy Violation

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Did not obtain permission from investigative personnel to give permission to the
on-scene members to turn their Wearable Camera Systems (WCS) off before the Force
Investigation Team arrived on scene to begin the formal investigative process (General Police
Order 4.06.04) – Failed to supervise subordinates (General Police Orders 2.02.05, 3.4.09,
4.2.06, 8.2.05, 3.2.14, 3.4.14, 2.01.03, and Manual of Rules 1.06) – Failed to complete quarterly
Wearable Camera System (WCS) audits (General Police Order 4.06.04) – Failed to report an
allegation of misconduct when a male told him that he was punched in the face by an officer
(General Police Order 1.07.05) – Failed to investigate an alleged pursuit, failed to report an
allegation of misconduct, failed to ensure a Stop Form was completed, and failed to ensure his
interview of a male was captured on video. This non-action constituted a gross neglect of duty
(Manual of Rules 2.14) – Failed to report an allegation of misconduct to the Internal Affairs Unit
when a male complained multiple times about a finger being in his rectum and his rights being
violated (General Police Order 1.07.05) – Used force on a male and failed to report it (General
Police Order 2.01.05) – Witnessed and became aware of a strip search performed by a Detective
and failed to explain to the individual the reason for the search and give the individual the
opportunity to voluntarily produce the suspected item and also failed to complete a pre-approved
form for strip searches (General Police Order 2.02.04) – Failed to supervise subordinates in
completing Stop Forms, properly reporting and documenting a use of force, properly conducting
and reporting a strip search, and failed to report an allegation of misconduct. This constituted a
gross neglect of duty (Manual of Rules 2.14) – Failed to call EMS in a timely manner (Manual of
Rules 4.14) –Discourteous comments (Manual of Rules 5.09) – Became aware that a male was
having difficulty breathing after a use of de minimus force and failed to obtain medical
assistance (General Police Order 2.01.03 and Manual of Rules 4.14) – Was informed that a male
was allegedly choked, kneed, punched, and his head was pushed into the ground and failed to
immediately report the incident to a supervisor or the Internal Affairs Unit and completed a use
of force investigation that did not address the involved citizen's allegations (General Police
Order 1.07.05) – Observed an injury to a male after a use of force was used by members and
failed to obtain professional medical care from EMS or convey the male for medical treatment
causing a delay in medical treatment (General Police Order 2.01.03 and Manual of Rules 4.14) –
Failed to properly handle evidence by storing the evidence in an vacant office (×7) (Manual of Rules 2.14, 4.03, 4.06 and Bureau of Special Services Investigative Unit Manual) – Failed to properly handle 19 shell casings that were turned over to him for evidence, storing the evidence
in a vacant office causing a Property Found report to be generated (Manual of Rules 2.14, 4.03,
4.06 and Bureau of Special Services Investigative Unit Manual) – Failed to properly handle
United States currency that is associated with the Expense Fund that is regulated by City of
Cleveland Codified Ordinance 135.091, by leaving the money in a desk drawer of a vacant
office, and not returning the money to the Budget Unit or the Fund Custodian for safe keeping
(Manual of Rules 2.14, 4.03, 4.06, Bureau of Special Services Investigative Unit Manual and
Expense Fund for Chief of Police).

Disposition: The charges of failing to explain to an individual the reason for a strip search,
failing to call EMS, and failing to properly handle United States currency were dismissed. The
two charges of failing to report an allegation of misconduct, and using force on a male and
failing to report it more closely comports with a Group II Violation (conduct that is contrary to
the values of the Division, or that interferes with its mission, operations or professional image, or
that involves a demonstrable serious risk to officer or public safety), the charges were amended
from Group III violations to Group II violations. The charge of failing to obtain medical
assistance more closely comports with a Group I Violation (conduct that has a negative impact
on the operations or professional image of the Division or negatively impacts relationships with
other officers, agencies or the public), the charge was amended from a Group II violation to a
Group I violation. Found “Guilty” of four Group I violations, twelve Group II violations and one
Group III violation. Considering the officer’s active discipline history (none), the officer’s
appointment as Commander is revoked and he shall return to his civil service rank of Sergeant
and receive a 30-day suspension. This corrective action is within the Group III violation range of
the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (supervisory or command rank of the officer,
multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to
the maximum group range) outweigh mitigating factors (none). In addition, the officer received a
Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction for not obtaining permission from the investigative
personnel to give permission to the on-scene members to turn their Wearable Camera Systems
off, failing to complete quarterly Wearable Camera System audits and failing to obtain medical
assistance for a male having difficulty breathing.
Rationale: Both charges of failing to supervise subordinates and failing to: investigate an
alleged pursuit, report an allegation of misconduct, ensure a Stop Form was completed, and
ensure an interview of a male was captured on video charges were merged into other
specifications to reduce duplicative charges. A review of the incident indicates that a "strip
search" did not occur and the officer was notified of the suspect's status, conducted an evaluation
of the suspect, and initiated the call to EMS. The officer was removed from the Unit, which did
not afford him the ability to provide proper notification concerning the status of the Unit money,
nor was an inventory of the contents of the office conducted at the time of his removal. The
officer did give permission to on-scene members to turn their Wearable Camera Systems (WCS)
off before the Force Investigation Team arrived to begin a formal investigation. The officer
failed to report an allegation of Misconduct when he was told by a complainant that he was
punched in the face by an officer and when he was told of an allegation of an improper strip
search. The officer failed to report that force was used on a complainant and failed to obtain
medical attention for an individual who was having difficulty breathing after the use of de-
minimus force by detectives when a citizen informed him that he was allegedly choked, kneed,
punched, and his head was pushed into the ground. He was discourteous to a male when he
stated: “Stop it dummy,” when the male was having a medical emergency and failed to obtain
medical care from EMS or convey the citizen for medical treatment, causing a delay in treatment. Additionally, the officer failed to properly handle evidence on multiple occasions and
failed to complete quarterly Wearable Camera System (WCS) audits.
Charges: WCS Violation,Failure to Report/Intervene (UOF),Improper Search/Frisk,Evidence Collection Violation,Failed to Request Medical Attention,Use of Force Violation,Failed to De-escalate,Offensive Remarks,Failure to Supervise,Lack of Service,Neglect of Duty

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Improperly had a vehicle towed and did not have a warrant to enter the curtilage
required under General Police Order 2.02.02 and Collins v. Virginia, 138 S. Ct. 1663 – Supreme
Court 2018. Additionally, the officer did not establish probable cause that the vehicle was used
in a crime, did not obtain a warrant to search the vehicle prior to it being processed and the
vehicle was held longer than seventy-two hours for processing (General Police Order 4.01.01
and Manual of Rules 2.04) – Failed to place the Wearable Camera System (WCS) into event
mode during the process tow of a vehicle (General Police Order 4.06.04) – Improperly had a
vehicle towed and did not have probable cause that the vehicle was used in a crime, it was
unlikely to contain evidence sought due to the length of time before the tow occurred and the
vehicle was held longer than seventy-two hours for processing without a court order (General
Police Order 4.01.01 and Manual of Rules 2.04).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of three Group I violations. Considering the officer’s active
discipline history (none), he received a Written Reprimand. In addition, the officer received a
Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction for the WCS violation. This corrective action is within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no active
discipline) outweigh aggravating factors (multiple violations).

Rationale: The officer was notified of the location of a suspect vehicle and found a vehicle with
damage believed to be consistent as reported, in a parking area shared by two properties. Upon
finding the vehicle, the officer received permission from a superior officer to process tow the
vehicle and did not get a search warrant for the vehicle even as the vehicle was held for over
seventy-two hours before the Crime Scene and Records Unit searched it for evidence. The
officer also did not activate his Wearable Camera System during the process tow of this vehicle;
this is the officer’s first WCS violation.

Specification: Used a level 1 and level 2 use of force and failed to report the use of force
(General Police Order 2.01.05).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the officer’s active discipline
history (none), the officer received a Written Reprimand. This corrective action is within the
First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no active
discipline, was aware of the suspect’s scrapes and abrasions which may have occurred prior to
the use of force) outweigh aggravating factors (the failure in reporting presents an appearance of
impropriety). In addition, the officer shall attend retraining through the Training Section on Use
of Force Reporting.

Rationale: The suspect may have been injured prior to officer contact. The officer did use joint
manipulation, a control hold and his body-weight to control the suspect. This application of force
should have been reported to a supervisory officer. Even if the officer was unsure if it was a
reportable use of force, he should have requested a supervisor to ensure a report was not
required. This failure to report was magnified, as the suspect suffered a fracture, causing this to
be an unreported Level-3 use of force.
Charges: Failure to Report/Intervene (UOF),Use of Force Violation,Appearance of Impropriety

Failed to generate a Crisis Intervention Report and complete a Crisis
Intervention Team (CIT) stat sheet during a call for service where a male was in mental crisis

(General Police Order 5.11.03). Although the officer spent time on scene attempting to de-
escalate the male in crisis and did request that a supervisor come to the scene, ultimately the

officer did not complete a report and a CIT stat sheet as required. Found “Guilty” of one Group I
violation. Because this is the officer’s fourth sustained Group I violation within a three year
period, the discipline automatically progresses to the next higher group, placing this corrective
action in the Group II range. Considering the officer’s active discipline history (11/26/2019 -
thirty day suspension), the officer received a seven day suspension. This corrective action is
within the First Group II violation range of the discipline matrix where aggravating factors
(active discipline history, CIT certified officer at the time of the incident) outweigh mitigating
factors (requested that a supervisor come to the scene).

patient who had a leg injury to stand up unexpectedly and hop on one leg out of the emergency
room lobby and into an unfamiliar area of the city without a ride home (General Police Order
1.1.03 and Manual of Rules 5.01, 5.08 and 5.09). The Group II violation was amended to a
Group I violation. Found “Guilty” of three Group I violations. Considering the officer’s active
discipline history (5/24/2021 - one day suspension), the officer received a three day suspension. This corrective action is within the Second Group I violation range of the discipline matrix
where aggravating factors (active discipline, multiple violations) outweigh mitigating factors
(none).
