within the first Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors
(liability to the City and was aware of General Police Order 1.02.13 via Divisional Notice 2023-
356 issued November 29, 2023) outweigh mitigating factors (accepted responsibility, no active
discipline).

Rationale: The member pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The member did work
secondary employment without prior approval as required.

Specification: Failed to obtain medical attention (General Police Order 2.01.03 and Manual of
Rules 4.14).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a two-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (level
of injury to juvenile victim) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline).

Rationale: The member failed to acknowledge an arrested juvenile’s complaint or recognize the
need for a medical evaluation. The juvenile was conveyed to the Cuyahoga County Juvenile
Center where he was not accepted due to his injury, causing a delay in medical treatment. The
member resigned from the Cleveland Division of Police prior to a disposition being issued for
this charge, he re-applied to the Cleveland Division of Police and was reinstated by the Civil
Service Commission and the Chief Director of Public Safety.

Specifications: Was cited for Operating Vehicle Under the Influence of Alcohol or Drugs (OVI)
(Manual of Rules 2.01, 2.02, 5.01 and 5.08) – Possession of a personal firearm while intoxicated
and in a motor vehicle (Manual of Rules 2.01, 2.02, 5.01, 5.08 and 5.13).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group II violation and one Group III violation. Considering
the member’s active discipline history (none) the member received a 15-day suspension. This
corrective action is within the First Group III Violation range of the discipline matrix when
mitigating factors (accepted responsibility, no active discipline) outweigh aggravating factors
(multiple violations).

Rationale: The member pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. Following a motor
vehicle accident, while off-duty, the member was operating his personally owned motor vehicle
while intoxicated and in possession of his personal firearm.

Specifications: Was cited for Driving or Physical Control While Under the Influence of Alcohol
or Drugs (OVI) (Manual of Rules 2.01, 2.02) – Possession of open containers of alcohol in a
motor vehicle (Manual of Rules 2.01, 2.02).

Disposition: Because her position as a Bi-lingual Communication Specialist is a non-sworn
position with the Cleveland Division of Police, the OVI charge more closely comports with a
Group I Violation (conduct that has a negative impact on the operations or professional image of
the Division or that negatively impacts relationships with other officers, agencies or the public),
the charge was amended from a Group II violation to a Group I violation. Found “Guilty” of two
Group I violations. Considering the member’s active discipline history (1/5/2024: two-day
suspension) the member received a five-day suspension. This discipline is within the Second
Group I violation range of the discipline matrix where aggravating factors (actions had the
potential to cause injury and harm to the public, active discipline, multiple violations) outweigh
mitigating factors (accepted partial responsibility).

Rationale: The member accepted partial responsibility. The member did operate a vehicle while
under the influence, and did have an open container of liquor in her vehicle.

Specification: Engaged in secondary employment without approval (General Police Order
1.02.13).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a one-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the first Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors
(liability to the City and was aware of General Police Order 1.02.13 via Divisional Notice 2023-
0356 issued November 29, 2023) outweigh mitigating factors (accepted responsibility, no active
discipline).

Rationale: The member pled “No Contest,” accepted responsibility and did engage in
unapproved secondary employment.

Specification: Failed to activate his Wearable Camera System (WCS) when responding to a
zone car involved in a motor vehicle crash (General Police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (4/30/2024: one-day suspension) the member received a one-day suspension.
This corrective action is appropriate for this Group I violation as mitigating factors (accepted
responsibility) outweigh aggravating factors (active discipline which occurred after this
infraction).

Rationale: The member pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The member did fail to
activate his Wearable Camera System during his investigative contact with the public. He has
previously failed to properly activate his Wearable Camera System; however, the corrective
action regarding that violation occurred after this incident.

Specification: Failed to activate his Wearable Camera System (WCS) prior to responding to a
call for service (General Police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (7/8/2022: one-day suspension) the member received a one-day suspension.
This corrective action is appropriate for this Group I violation as mitigating factors (active
discipline is for a dissimilar Group I violation) outweigh aggravating factors (active discipline).

Rationale: The member’s Wearable Camera System indicates that it was not properly activated,
and was only in buffering mode, which does not include audio, and in this instance did not
capture the entire use of force. However, his partner’s Wearable Camera System was activated
during the entire incident.

Hearing Held By: Deputy Chief of Homeland Special Operations
Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Tardy (×2) & Absent Without Leave (AWOL) (Manual of Rules 10.01) –
Refused to handle a call for Spanish to English translation (×3) (Manual of Rules 2.03) – Using a
personal cell phone while on the Public Safety Communications Center (PSCC) floor without
permission (Bureau of Communications and Property Control Policy and Procedures General
Communications Control Section 1.3.01) – Call taking violation (held incident) (Bureau of
Communications and Property Control Policy and Procedures General Call Taking Procedures
1.1.17).

Disposition: The AWOL charge more closely comports with a Group I Violation (conduct that
has a negative impact on the operations or professional image of the Division or that negatively
impacts relationships with other officers, agencies, or the public). The charge was amended from
a Group II violation to a Group I violation. Found “Guilty” of six Group I violations.
Considering the member’s active discipline history (none), the member received a three-day
suspension. This corrective action is within the Group I violation range of the discipline matrix
when aggravating factors (multiple violations) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline).

Rationale: The member pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The member was tardy
on two occasions (car difficulties) and AWOL on one occasion (did not ensure her phone was
charging, causing the alarm to fail). There is no prior discipline regarding tardiness or AWOL.
The member was on the Public Safety Communications Center (PSCC) floor, on a personal
phone call without supervisory approval, and refused three calls requiring assistance in Spanish
to English translation. Finally, the member remained on a phone call for an unnecessarily long
time delaying the member from answering incoming calls for service. Multiple violations arising
from a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range.
Charges: Absent Without Leave (AWOL),Tardiness,Failed to Provide Language Services,Telecommunications Violation

Hearing Held By: Deputy Chief of Homeland Special Operations
Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Failed to report an act of misconduct by another member to a supervisor or the
Internal Affairs Unit (General Police Order 1.07.05).

Disposition: The charge more closely comports with a Group I Violation (conduct that has a
negative impact on the operations or professional image of the Division or that negatively
impacts relationships with other officers, agencies or the public), the charge was amended from a
Group II violation to a Group I violation. Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering
the member’s active discipline history (none) the member received a one-day suspension. This
corrective action is within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (delays in reporting can lead to the loss of crucial evidence) outweigh
mitigating factors (no active discipline, accepted responsibility).

Rationale: The member did fail to report an allegation of criminal misconduct for two days, and
while this delay did not hinder the investigation, in other instances a delay may have. The
member was off-duty when he was notified and did report the incident to his supervisor on the
next tour of duty when he and his supervisor were working.

Hearing Held By: Deputy Chief of Homeland Special Operations
Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Failed to safeguard and secure city-issued Cleveland Police Identification card
(General Police Order 6.04.03 and Manual of Rules 6.05) – Worked unauthorized secondary
employment (General Police Order 1.02.13).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of two Group I violations. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a one-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (multiple
violations, liability to the City and was aware of General Police Order 1.02.13 via Divisional
Notice 2023-0356 issued November 29, 2023) outweigh mitigating factors (accepted
responsibility, no active discipline). In addition, the member shall reimburse the Cleveland
Division of Police for the Loss of City Property.

Rationale: The member pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The member did work
secondary employment at Dante’s Inferno without prior authorization and did fail in the
safekeeping of his city-issued Cleveland Police Identification card. Multiple violations arising
from a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range.

Hearing Held By: Deputy Chief of Homeland Special Operations
Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Did send an inappropriate picture to an Explorer Advisor via text message, while
off-duty (General Police Order 1.1.03 and Manual of Rules 5.01, 5.08 and 5.09).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a one-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (picture was
so distasteful that it led to the resignation of the individual) outweigh mitigating factors (no
active discipline).

Rationale: The member did send an unwelcome and disparaging picture (Princess Fiona in ogre
form, from a Shrek movie) via text to an Explorer Advisor, which was so disrespectful, it left her
deeply disheartened and led to the tendering of her resignation.

Specification: Failed to furnish name to an arrested male at the County Jail when requested
(General Police Order 1.07.04 and Manual of Rules 4.13) – Discourteous comments (General
Police Order 1.1.03, Manual of Rules 5.08 and 5.09) – Failed to use proper escort techniques
(Manual of Rules 4.01).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of three Group I violations. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (2/6/2024: Written Reprimand) the member received a three-day suspension.
This corrective action is within the Second Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (multiple violations, active discipline) outweigh mitigating factors (accepted
partial responsibility).

Rationale: The member accepted partial responsibility. The member did fail to furnish his name
when requested, only stating his badge number and did act unprofessionally when he told the
complainant that he would allow him to call his mother and then changed his mind because,
“Well yeah, cause you were being shitty with me.” The member also mocked the complainant
when he stated, “Hurt all over, hurt all over,” referring to the complainant telling the nurse that
he hurts all over. He also failed to use a proper escort technique on the arrested male,
contributing to the prisoner falling, causing damage to his prosthetic leg. Multiple violations
arising from a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum
group range.

Specification: Posted disparaging comments on the Independence Police Department’s
Facebook social media page (General Police Order 7.03.03 and Manual of Rules 2.01, 4.12,
5.01, 5.02, 5.08 and 5.09).

Disposition: The charge more closely comports with a Group I Violation (conduct that has a
negative impact on the operations or professional image of the Division or that negatively
impacts relationships with other officers, agencies or the public), the charge was amended from a
Group II violation to a Group I violation. Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering
the member’s active discipline history (none) the member received a one-day suspension. This
corrective action is within the Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating
factors (a good relationship with other agencies is imperative, especially one which members of
the Cleveland Division of Police may find themselves working with – task force, etc.) outweigh
mitigating factors (no active discipline).

Rationale: The member did post on the Independence Police Department’s Facebook social
media page a post which was offensive to the chief of the Independence Police Department. In
his post, the member equated Independence Police officers to “security guards at best.” Further,
he accused the Independence Police Department of “fleecing” his son over tow and impound fees
and stated, “Pathetic people pretending to be cops. More like lying thieves.”

Specification: Absent Without Leave (AWOL) and asleep on-duty (Manual of Rules 5.12).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group II violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (9/1/2021: 7-day suspension, 2/22/2023: 9-day suspension) the member
received a ten-day suspension. This corrective action is within the Second Group II violation
range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (active and similar misconduct)
outweigh mitigating factors (accepted responsibility).

Rationale: The member accepted responsibility. She was 32 minutes late returning from her
combined lunch and break – she was found sleeping in her vehicle in the parking lot when she
should have been at her station and has previous and active discipline for being asleep on-duty.

Specifications: Arrested for Operating Vehicle Under the Influence of Alcohol or Drugs (OVI)
by the Rocky River Police Department (Manual of Rules 2.01) – Failed to notify
Communications Control Section (CCS) after being the subject of a criminal investigation
(General Police Order 1.07.05).

Disposition: The charge of failing to notify CCS was dismissed. Found “Guilty” of one Group II
violation. Considering the member’s active discipline history (4/19/2024: 6-day suspension) the
member received a ten-day suspension. This corrective action is within the Second Group II
violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (active discipline) outweigh
mitigating factors (none).

Rationale: The member accepted partial responsibility. He pled “No Contest” and was found
guilty of an amended charge of Having Physical Control of Vehicle while under the Influence
and he did call his supervisor to notify him of the arrest. While the General Police Order states
that he was to notify the Communications Control Section, who would in turn notify an on-duty
supervisor, his call to his supervisor, while not the correct procedure, was an attempt by the
member to obey the spirit of the policy – that the Division becomes aware of his arrest.

Specifications: Engaged in conduct that would constitute the crime of Assault (Ohio Revised
Code §2903.13 and Manual of Rules 2.01) – Engaged in conduct that would constitute the crime
of Disorderly Conduct (Ohio Revised Code §2917.11 and Manual of Rules 2.01) – Engaged in
conduct unbecoming a member of the Cleveland Division of Police (Manual of Rules 4.12, 4.20,
5.08, 5.09).

Disposition: The charges of engaging in conduct that would constitute the crime of Assault and
engaging in conduct unbecoming a member of the Cleveland Division of Police were dismissed.
The charge of engaging in conduct that would constitute the crime of Disorderly Conduct more
closely comports with a Group I Violation (conduct that has a negative impact on the operations
or professional image of the Division or that negatively impacts relationships with other officers,
agencies or the public); the charge was amended from a Group II violation to a Group I violation.
Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active discipline history
(none), the member received a two-day suspension. This corrective action is within the First
Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (maliciousness of the
disorderly conduct) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline).

Rationale: The member was off-duty and did not identify herself as a police officer. The charge
of engaging in conduct that would constitute the crime of Assault is duplicative of engaging in
conduct that would constitute the crime of Disorderly Conduct; Disorderly Conduct more closely
fits the member’s behavior.
Charges: Arrest / Criminal Charge,Conduct Unbecoming / Diminished Esteem

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Unauthorized secondary employment (General Police Order 1.02.13).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none), the member received a one-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (liability to
the City, aware of General Police Order 1.02.13 via Divisional Notice 2023-0356 issued
November 29, 2023) outweigh mitigating factors (accepted responsibility, no active discipline).

Rationale: The member pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The member did engage
in unauthorized secondary employment at Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse.

Specifications: Taunting and disrespectful language (Manual of Rules 5.09) – Unwarranted call
to a Cleveland Division of Police (CDP) supervisor console (language was disrespectful and
insolent) (Manual of Rules 2.06) – Sick leave abuse (×2) (failed to notify a supervisor) (General
Police Order 1.3.24) – Arrested for Operating Vehicle Under the Influence of Alcohol or Drugs
(OVI) (Cleveland Codified Ordinance §433.021 and Manual of Rules 2.01) – Cited for driver
and passenger seatbelt (Cleveland Codified Ordinance §433.021 and Manual of Rules 2.01) –
Arrested for Menacing (Manual of Rules 2.01) – Retaliatory statements towards
arresting/conveying officers (General Police Order 1.07.07) – Diminished the esteem (×3)
(Manual of Rules 5.01).
Disposition: The charges of being disrespectful and insolent during an unwarranted call to a
CDP supervisor console and being cited for driver and passenger seatbelts were dismissed. The
menacing arrest charge more closely comports with a Group II Violation (conduct that is
contrary to the values of the Division, or that interferes with its mission, operations or
professional image, or that involves a demonstrable serious risk to officer or public safety); the
charge was amended from a Group III violation to a Group II violation. Found “Guilty” of one
Group I violation and seven Group II violations. Considering the member’s active discipline
history (none) the member received an eight-day suspension. This corrective action is within the
Group II Violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (multiple violations
arising from a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum
group range) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline).
Rationale: The charges of taunting and disrespectful language, and disrespectful and insolent
language during an unwarranted call to a CDP supervisor console are closely related and merged
into one Group I violation. The two charges being arrested for OVI and being cited for driver and
passenger seatbelt are closely related and merged into one Group II violation. The member failed
to notify the Communications Control Section regarding her absence following her arrest and
believed a patrol supervisor would make the notification. Based on the members arrest for
Menacing, there is clear and convincing evidence that she made threats to inflict physical harm
to her neighbors in violation of the Cleveland Codified Ordinance for Menacing. While under
this arrest she made retaliatory statements to the arresting/conveying officers, but because her
statements were made in a highly intoxicated state and not supported by retaliatory action the
charge more closely aligns with Conduct Unbecoming. The member identified herself as a
Cleveland Dispatcher to Lakewood Police Officers during her OVI arrest, she refused to exit her
vehicle and screamed over the officer while he attempted to read her Miranda Rights. During a
call with a Lakewood Police Dispatcher on a recorded line the member stated: “No, I was not
thinking of Cleveland. Bitch, I know," and followed up by saying, “Bitch, I am very
knowledgeable. I do it for a living.” While engaged with Cleveland Division of Police officers
during a call for service in which she was the suspect, she refused to provide her identity to the
officers, yelled and spoke over them, and made retaliatory comments to the arresting officers, all
of which occurred after she identified herself as a Cleveland Division of Police Dispatcher. The
aforementioned conduct would reasonably tend to diminish the esteem of the Division of Police
in the eyes of the public. Multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate
the disciplinary action to the maximum group range.
Charges: Offensive Remarks,Sick Leave Abuse,Unprofessional Conduct,Arrest / Criminal Charge,Conduct Unbecoming / Diminished Esteem

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Refused to take a Code 2 assignment given by Cleveland Police Radio Dispatch
(Manual of Rules 7.01) – Failed to follow lawful order (Manual of Rules 2.02, 2.06 and 4.08) –
Insubordination (yelled and argued with superior officers) (Manual of Rules 2.06).
Disposition: The charge of failing to follow a lawful order was dismissed. The insubordination
charge more closely comports with a Group II Violation (conduct that is contrary to the values of
the Division, or that interferes with its mission, operations or professional image, or that involves
a demonstrable serious risk to officer or public safety); the charge was amended from a Group III
violation to a Group II violation. Found “Guilty” of two Group II violations. Considering the
member’s active discipline history (none) the member received a seven-day suspension. This
corrective action is within the First Group II Violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate the
disciplinary action to the maximum group range) outweigh mitigating factors (no active
discipline).
Rationale: Cleveland Police Radio Dispatch attempted to give the member an assignment,
which she initially did not accept. Eventually, she did agree to take the assignment, but during
the delay it had been assigned to another officer. Evidence presented at the hearing was
conflicting regarding whether the member was actually in her patrol car at the time of the
supervisory order regarding this assignment. The member did raise her voice, yell and argue with superior officers. Multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate the
disciplinary action to the maximum group range.
Charges: Neglect of Duty,Lack of Service,Arrest / Criminal Charge,Insubordination

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Tardy (×2) (failed to report for work at his scheduled time) (Manuel of Rules
10.01) – Sick leave abuse (×2) (called in sick without notifying the Interim Commander or
Lieutenant) (scheduled to attend Code Red/ Active Shooter Drills) (General Police Order 1.3.06)
– Disobeyed a direct order (Manuel of Rules 2.02, 2.06 and 2.07).
Disposition: Both sick leave abuse charges and the disobeying a direct order charge were
dismissed. Found “Guilty” of two Group I violations. Considering the member’s active discipline
history (none) the member received a two-day suspension. This corrective action is within the
First Group I Violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (multiple
violations, supervisory rank) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline, accepted
responsibility).
Rationale: The member accepted responsibility for the tardy charges and had legitimate medical
issues for the use of sick time. The member was given a direct order to personally investigate an
unusually high number of “No Lunch Cards” being forwarded from Patrol, however, the email
requiring an investigation into “No Lunch” cards was directed to several supervisors in the chain
of command, who then initiated the investigation and submitted their conclusions prior to the
member reporting for duty and without him delegating that task to them. Multiple violations
arising from a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum
group range.

Specification: Intoxicated while on-duty (Manual of Rules 5.04 and 5.06).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group III violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a ten-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the First Group III Violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no
active discipline) outweigh aggravating factors (none). In addition, the member shall complete an
Employee Assistance Unit program.
Rationale: The member engaged in a discussion with a supervisor who noticed the member had
slurred speech, glassy and bloodshot eyes, smelled of alcohol and was more engaging and
talkative than normal. Based on their observations, the member was ordered to submit to an
emergency alcohol/drug test. The results of the test confirmed “positive” for alcohol.
Charges: Alcohol or Smoking on Duty / In Uniform

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Refused mandatory overtime on eight separate occasions (Bureau of
Communications and Property Control Policy and Procedure; Mandatory Overtime Refusal
1.3.07, Manual of Rules 10.01).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group II violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a five-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the Bureau of Communications Mandatory Overtime Refusal Discipline Matrix violation
range (7 or 8 refusals leads to a 5 to 10-day suspension) when mitigating factors (no active
discipline, provided staff shortage relief by coming in early on several occasions) outweigh
aggravating factors (none).
Rationale: The member did refuse mandatory overtime 14 times during the Fourth Quarter of
2024; however, she did make up six mandatory overtime refusals during the Quarter, leaving
eight net refusals in the Fourth Quarter of 2024.

Specification: Underreported a Level 3 Use of Force incident (General Police Order 2.01.05).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (7/18/2022: 2-day suspension) the member received a three-day suspension.
This corrective action is within the Second Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (active discipline, a delay in the investigation may create an appearance of
impropriety regarding a use of force incident) outweigh mitigating factors (suspect was highly
combative, the force used was within policy).
Rationale: The Wearable Camera System (WCS) video showed that the member did have the
suspect in a neck hold while he was attempting to gain control of a suspect. The suspect did
physically attack the member and the force was found to be within policy. However, there is a
distinction in investigations regarding a Level-2 use of force versus a Level-3 use of force, in
that a Use of Force Investigation Team is notified to investigate the Level-3 use of force. Failure
to immediately notify the Use of Force Investigation Team can create the appearance of
impropriety regarding the investigation.

Specification: Abusive and demeaning language (Manual of Rules 5.09).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group II violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (1/15/2024: 13-day suspension) the member received an eight-day suspension.
This corrective action is within the First Group II violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (active discipline, supervisory rank) outweigh mitigating factors
(immediately self-reported, accepted responsibility).
Rationale: The member accepted responsibility and did use abusive and demeaning language
towards an arrested male.

Specification: Unauthorized secondary employment (General Police Order 1.02.13).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a one-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors
(liability to the City and was aware of General Police Order 1.02.13 via Divisional Notice 2023-
0356 issued November 29, 2023) outweigh mitigating factors (none).
Rationale: The member did engage in unauthorized secondary employment.

Specification: Wearable Camera System (WCS) violation (failed to place into event mode)
(General Police Order 4.06.04).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (11/21/2024: 3-day suspension) the member received a one-day suspension.
This corrective action is within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (dissimilar active Group I discipline) outweigh mitigating factors (nature of
the assignment – officer needing help with large fight).
Rationale: The member did fail to place his Wearable Camera System (WCS) into event mode
prior to responding to a call for service.

Specification: Refused mandatory overtime (×9) (Bureau of Communications and Property
Control Policy and Procedure; Mandatory Overtime Refusal 1.3.07 and Manual of Rules 10.01).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group III violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a 10-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the Bureau of Communications and Property Control Communications Control Section
Mandatory Overtime Refusal matrix (nine to ten refusals equates to a ten to fifteen day
suspension).

Rationale: The member refused to work mandatory overtime on sixteen dates in the Fourth
Quarter of 2024. However, she worked overtime on seven dates, which each nullify one
mandatory overtime refusal in the Fourth Quarter of 2024.

Specifications: Asleep on-duty (×2) (Manual of Rules 5.12) – Call-taking violation (Manual of
Rules 4.03 and Bureau of Communications and Property Control Communication Control
Section 1.1.17) – Refused mandatory overtime (×3) (Bureau of Communications and Property
Control Policy and Procedure; Mandatory Overtime Refusal 1.3.07 and Manual of Rules 10.01).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of two Group I violations and two Group II violations. Considering
the member’s active discipline history (none) the member received a six-day suspension. This
corrective action is within the First Group II violation range of the discipline matrix when
mitigating factors (no active discipline, accepted responsibility, reported for duty early to help
with staff shortage, took action to stay awake) outweigh aggravating factors (multiple violations).

Rationale: The member did fall asleep twice during a 16-hour workday (she did report for duty
four hours early to help with a staff shortage). She also made several critical errors on a phone
call (CAD: 2025-018367) with the errors occurring between the two times she fell asleep, and as
such are indicative of her tiredness and not indifference to her duties. The member did eventually
stand up (as opposed to being seated) during her shift to keep herself awake, and immediately
took responsibility for her failure to stay awake. Additionally, she did refuse mandatory overtime
a net three times during the Third Quarter of 2024. Multiple violations arising from a single
event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range.
Charges: Refusal of Mandatory Overtime,Asleep On-Duty,Neglect of Duty

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Discourteous speech (Manual of Rules 5.01 and 5.09) – Discarded a State of
Ohio identification card (Manual of Rules 4.01) – Profane language (Manual of Rules Section
5.09).

Disposition: The discourteous charge more closely comports with a Group I Violation (conduct
that has a negative impact on the operations or professional image of the Division or that
negatively impacts relationships with other officers, agencies or the public); the charge was
amended from a Group II violation to a Group I violation. Found “Guilty” of three Group I
violations. Considering the member’s active discipline history (none) the member received a
three-day suspension. This corrective action is within the First Group I violation range of the
discipline matrix when aggravating factors (multiple violations, Written Reprimand issued on
7/17/2024 for discourteous language) outweigh mitigating factors (none).

Rationale: The member told a citizen to “Go ahead, I don’t give a fuck if you file a complaint”
and made a statement “Get the fuck out of here,” and on another occasion he used profane
language when arresting a combative and intoxicated female saying, “We are in the middle of the
fucking road.” The member’s use of language is outside of Division expectations and is not
acceptable. He also threw a State of Ohio identification card into the trash shortly after receiving
it from another member. Multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate
the disciplinary action to the maximum group range.

Specifications: Engaged publicly in a disparaging conversation (Manual of Rules 5.02) –
Discourteous in speech (Manual of Rules 5.09).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of two Group I violations. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a three-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors
(multiple violations) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline).

Rationale: The member did engage in a disparaging conversation with a congressperson after a
police awards event. This was not the time nor the place to have this interaction. His behavior
became loud enough to draw the attention of an Interim Commander, who upon observing his
discourteous speech towards the congressperson remfound it necessary to have him removed from
the event. Multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate the
disciplinary action to the maximum group range.

Hearing Held By: Deputy Chief of Homeland Special Operations
Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Call taking violation (×2) (argumentative, rude, disrespectful) (unprofessional,
discourteous) (Manual of Rules 5.08, 5.09 and Communications Control Section 1.1.17).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of two Group I violations. Considering the member’s active

day suspension, 3/31/2025: 13-day suspension) the member received a seven-day suspension.
Three or more sustained violations occurring in the same group within a three year period
automatically progresses the discipline into the next higher group. This corrective action is
within the First Group II violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors
(multiple violations, active discipline) outweigh mitigating factors (none).

Rationale: The member has repeatedly failed to demonstrate courtesy and respect in her speech
while taking calls for service. This is particularly concerning as her duty as a Safety Telephone

Hearing Held By: Deputy Chief of Homeland Special Operations
Charge & Discipline Decision:

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active

(liability to the City and was aware of General Police Order 1.02.13 via Divisional Notice 24-
393 issued December 4, 2024) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline).

Rationale: The member did engage in unauthorized secondary employment at the Jack Casino.

Specifications: Failed to search a male incident to arrest (General Police Order 3.01.01 and

Conducted a traffic stop contrary to training received from the Cleveland Division of Police
Academy (General Police Orders 2.02.01 and Manual of Rules 2.03 and 2.04).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of three Group I violations and one Group II violation. Considering
the member’s active discipline history (none) the member received a six-day suspension. This
corrective action is within the First Group II violation range of the discipline matrix when
mitigating factors (no active discipline, accepted responsibility) outweigh aggravating factors
(multiple violations). In addition, the member shall be retrained by the Training Section in
vehicle stops, “Terry” frisks and searches incident to arrest.

Rationale: The member was acting as a Field Training Officer (FTO) and did not immediately
take custody of a hit-skip suspect with an active traffic warrant, and his lack of diligence allowed
the suspect to leave the scene in another vehicle. Upon catching this vehicle (without a pursuit),
the member pulled up alongside the vehicle and his Probationary Patrol Officer (PPO) instructed
the driver to pull over – this maneuver is not a trained tactic as it places himself and especially
his partner in a dangerous position. Upon being ordered to arrest the hit-skip suspect with an
active traffic warrant, the member handcuffed him and placed him into the back seat of his patrol
car without performing a “Terry” frisk for weapons or a search incident to arrest. Later, a
Sergeant searched the suspect, and the suspect was armed with a loaded firearm and a knife.
Further, the member did not complete the required “Stop Form” for this incident.

Specification: Unauthorized secondary employment (General Police Order 1.02.13).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a one-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the first Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors
(supervisory rank, liability to the City and was aware of General Police Order 1.02.13 via

Rationale: The member engaged in unauthorized secondary employment at Greenbridge
Consulting LLC when the publicly accessible website for Greenbridge Consulting LLC listed his

identified him as a member of the “Cleveland Division of Police” who is “currently serving as
the Bureau of Compliance Commander.” Further, the member’s biographical information stated
that his “role at Greenbridge Consulting centers on guiding clients through transformative
changes that prioritize compliance, community trust, and sustainable safety improvements."

Specification: Failed to notify Communications Control Section (CCS) after an off-duty Motor
Vehicle Accident (MVA) (Manual of Rules 4.20).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (11/3/2021: 8-day suspension, 9/16/2022: 10-day suspension, 11/4/2022: 20-
day suspension, 7/17/2023: 13-day suspension, 9/11/2024: 3-day suspension) the member
received a seven-day suspension. Three or more sustained violations occurring in the same
group within a three year period automatically progresses the discipline into the next higher
group. This corrective action is within the Group II violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (active discipline – including multiple sustained Group I violations)
outweigh mitigating factors (none).

Rationale: While off-duty, the member was involved in a motor vehicle crash in the City of
Cleveland and left the scene without notifying the Communications Control Section to have a

Charges: Preventable Motor Vehicle Accident (MVA),Failure to Report/Notify

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Level 3 force (not necessary, not proportional) (General Police Order 2.01.03) –
Discharged service weapon (General Police Order 2.01.03).
Disposition: The charge of discharging his service weapon was merged into the Level 3 force
charge. Found “Guilty” of one Group III violation. Considering the member’s active discipline
history (none), the member received a fifteen-day suspension. This corrective action is within the
first Group III Violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (multiple
violations, actual or demonstrable legal or financial risk to the Division or the City as he fired
his weapon after the imminent threat had passed and with multiple houses as the backdrop)
outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline, believed gunfire was headed in his direction,
very rapidly evolving situation requiring split-second decision making). In addition, the member will receive training regarding the prohibited use of deadly force towards a motor vehicle when a
direct threat, outside of a vehicle, is not identified.
Rationale: The charge of discharging his service weapon is a result of the action noted in the
Level 3 force charge; they were merged. The member and his partner were gathering information
from a radio assignment of threats – suspect on scene, and during his interview of the
complainant, the complainant indicated that she believed that damage to her daughter’s vehicle
came from a bullet striking the vehicle’s windshield. The complainant proceeded to show the
member broken glass from the reportedly shot-out car window in the driveway of her residence,
and advised that she did not know the whereabouts of her daughter, who was not at the
residence. The complainant further expressed concern over the potential for harm to anyone in
her home by stating that her brother was sleeping on the couch and could have been shot if the
person she suspected had shot through the house. Just prior to clearing this call for service, a
large volley of gunshots was heard coming from the area approximately 500 feet from the
member’s location. Moments later, as a vehicle (Ford Fusion) was headed toward his location, a
second volley of gunshots was fired. The member reported that the combination of heavy gunfire
and the car’s approach toward his position led him to engage with his firearm. He further stated
that he waited to fire until the vehicle cleared his partner’s position in front of him before firing.
Video captured the member looking toward the sound of the gunfire and calling out “Shots
fired!” as he moved to the apron area of the driveway. Once the vehicle had passed his location,
he then discharged his City-issued pistol five times toward the vehicle, including while the
vehicle was at a significant distance from his position. The members actions, based on the
knowledge he reasonably had at the time, including firing upon a vehicle that was not a verified
and visible threat and had passed his position, was not necessary, proportional, or objectively
reasonable, and violated policy, creating a significant and unnecessary risk to others when he
discharged his weapon despite the residential backdrop, as shown by his round striking an
uninvolved occupied residence further down the street. Multiple violations arising from a single
event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range.
Charges: Use of Force Violation,Improper Handling of Firearm

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Unprofessional Behavior/Conduct (posted an image of himself in a Cleveland
Division of Police (CDP) uniform pointing at the sky with his firearm to social media) (posted a
video of himself shooting at the range to social media) (General Police Order 7.03.03 and
Manual of Rules 5.01, 5.08 and 5.13) – Unprofessional Behavior/Conduct (posted a video of an
accident scene to social media) (posted an image of Mobile Data Terminal (MDT) screen to
social media) (posted a Form-1 and images of simulation equipment to social media) (posted
images of recruits & instructors at the range to social media) (General Police Order 7.03.03) –
Unprofessional Behavior/Conduct (posted an image himself in uniform holding his firearm
invited racially insensitive comments) (Manual of Rules 5.01).
Disposition: The unprofessional behavior/conduct charge relating to posting an image of himself
in uniform holding his firearm that invited racially insensitive comments was dismissed. The
unprofessional behavior/conduct charges of posting images to social media that contained: a
portion of an MDT screen; a Form-1 and images of simulation equipment: faces of new recruits
and the instructors on the range: and a video of himself shooting at the range more closely
comports with Group I Violations (conduct that has a negative impact on the operations or professional image of the Division or that negatively impacts relationships with other officers,
agencies or the public); those charges were amended from Group II violations to Group I
violations. Found “Guilty” of five Group I violations and one Group II violation. Considering the
member’s active discipline history (none), the member received a nine-day suspension. This
corrective action is within the second Group II violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (active discipline, multiple violations arising from a single event or incident
may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range) outweigh mitigating factors
(none).
Rationale: There was insufficient evidence to support a determination that the member invited
racially insensitive comments to a social media post; as such, this charge was dismissed. Because
none of the posts contained offensive, abusive, or demeaning content, the violations regarding
the Cleveland Division of Police’s social media policy were amended to Group I violations. The
member was handling his firearm in a manner inconsistent with Division policy. Multiple
violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the
maximum group range.
Charges: Unprofessional Conduct,Telecommunications Violation,Improper Handling of Firearm,Offensive Remarks

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Continued interrogating and questioning a male after the male requested an
attorney (General Police Order 2.02.03).
Disposition: The charge more closely comports with a Group I Violation (conduct that has a
negative impact on the operations or professional image of the Division or that negatively
impacts relationships with other officers, agencies, or the public); the charge was amended from
a Group II violation to a Group I violation. Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering
the member’s active discipline history (none), the member received a one-day suspension. This
corrective action is within the first Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (Civil Rights violation) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline, he
was new to the Detective Unit).
Rationale: The member was relatively new to the Detective Unit. Another detective was
questioning the in-custody suspect when the suspect invoked his Miranda rights by requesting
legal representation, and that detective continued to question the suspect; the member took no
action to deter the detective from continuing to question the suspect. Further, the member also
asked questions after the suspect invoked his Miranda rights. Because any evidence elicited in
the violation of Miranda cannot be used against the suspect in court (i.e., a Civil Rights violation
that cannot actually harm the subject), this more closely comports with a Group I violation, and
as such, the charge was amended to a Group I violation.

Specification: Absent With Out Leave (AWOL) (Manual of Rules 10.01 and City of Cleveland
Human Resources Policies and Procedures, Workplace Policies: C-3).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group II violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (3/25/2025: 8-day suspension, 4/7/2025: Written Reprimand), the member
received a ten-day suspension. This corrective action is within the Second Group II violation
range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (active discipline: 3/25/2025 – 8-day
suspension, 4/7/2025 – Written Reprimand) outweigh mitigating factors (none).
Rationale: The member did fail to report for duty as scheduled. The Communications Control
Section had a zone car respond to her home and after the arrival of the zone car she notified radio
that she had just woken up and would attempt to find a ride. In a subsequent call, the member
stated that she was unable to find a ride and she was instructed she would be marked as AWOL
for that day.

Specifications: Arrested for Driving Under the Influence of Alcohol or Drugs (DUI) (M-1)
(Florida Statute 316.193 & Manual of Rules 2.01) – Arrested for speeding (Florida Statute
316.183 & Manual of Rules 2.01).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation and one Group II violation. Considering
the member’s active discipline history (none), the member received an eight-day suspension.
This corrective action is within the First Group II violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (action had the potential to cause injury and harm to the public) outweigh
mitigating factors (none).
Rationale: The member pled guilty to an amended offense (Florida statute) of reckless driving
when reduced from DUI–316.192(2) – a second-degree misdemeanor, and pled guilty to
speeding 20-29 miles per hour over the speed limit. Multiple violations arising from a single
event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range.

Specifications: Failed to advise radio dispatcher (Manual of Rules 4.10) – Failed to assist an
outside police agency (General Police Order 4.03.01) – Failed to act (Manual of Rules 4.11) –
Diminish the esteem (Manual of Rules 5.01) – Wearable Camera System (WCS) (failed to place
into event mode) (General Police Order 4.06.04) – Emergency Response Driving violation
(General Police Order 4.01.01).
Disposition: The emergency response driving charge was dismissed. The charges of failing to
assist an outside agency and failing to act were merged into one charge. The charges of failing to
advise radio, failing to assist an outside agency/failing to act and diminishing the esteem more
closely comport with Group I Violations (conduct that has a negative impact on the operations or
professional image of the Division or that negatively impacts relationships with other officers,
agencies or the public); the charges were amended from Group II violations to Group I
violations. Found “Guilty” of four Group I violations. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none), the member received a three-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors
(multiple violations) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline).

Rationale: The member was en route to a Priority-2 assignment when he came upon a multi-
vehicle accident that blocked the lanes of West Saint Clair Avenue between West 3rd and West

6
th Streets. Two Strongsville Police vehicles were on scene with their emergency overhead lights
activated. Instead of stopping to see if assistance was needed and notifying radio of this police
incident of a serious nature, the member or his partner activated the patrol car’s overhead
emergency lights and proceeded to make a U-turn and leave the scene. These actions were
covered in a story by Fox 8 news and would reasonably tend to diminish the esteem of the Division of Police or its personnel in the eyes of the public. At no time during his response to the
Priority-2 assignment did the member activate his Wearable Camera System. Because the
member and his partner were en route to a Priority-2 assignment, these actions, as noted, more
closely comport with Group I violations. The charges of failing to assist an outside agency and
failing to act are closely related and were merged into a single charge. The use of lights and
sirens relating to emergency response driving is dictated by Ohio Revised Code §4511.45, and
there is insufficient evidence to support a finding of guilt regarding the member’s response to the
Priority-2 assignment, and the charge was dismissed. Multiple violations arising from a single
event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range.
Charges: Failure to Report/Notify,Failed to Assist,Lack of Service,Conduct Unbecoming / Diminished Esteem,WCS Violation,Travel Violation

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Vehicle pursuit violation (General Police Order 3.2.02).
Disposition: The charge more closely comports with a Group I Violation (conduct that has a
negative impact on the operations or professional image of the Division or that negatively impacts relationships with other officers, agencies, or the public); the charge was amended from
a Group II violation to a Group I violation. Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering
the member’s active discipline history (none), the member received a one-day suspension. This
corrective action is within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (the existence of an actual or demonstrable legal or financial risk to the
Division or the City) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline).
Rationale: The member did proceed through multiple intersections at high speeds without the
siren activated. By continuing emergency driving without the siren, he was in conflict with Ohio
Revised Code §4511.45, which states in pertinent part, “a public safety vehicle... driver is giving
an audible signal by siren, exhaust whistle, or bell, no driver of any other vehicle shall fail to
yield the right-of-way...”

Specification: Abusive/demeaning language (Manual of Rules 5.09).
Disposition: The charge more closely comports with a Group I Violation (conduct that has a
negative impact on the operations or professional image of the Division or that negatively
impacts relationships with other officers, agencies, or the public); the charge was amended from
a Group II violation to a Group I violation. Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering
the member’s active discipline history (none) the member received a one-day suspension. This
corrective action is within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (the language was directed at the suspect, “you f***ing stupid”) outweigh
mitigating factors (no active discipline).
Rationale: The member had engaged in a level 2 use of force incident with a juvenile suspect
who had what appeared to be a pistol partially concealed under his pants. Upon gaining control
of the juvenile suspect and retrieving what he had determined to be a facsimile firearm, the
member stated to the juvenile suspect, “God damn, you f***ing stupid.” This speech is outside
division expectations and more closely comports with a Group I violation (discourtesy/rudeness)
than the Group II violation (abusive/demeaning language).

Specification: Walked a naked female into the University Hospital Emergency Room without
properly covering her (General Police Order 1.01.03 and Manual of Rules 5.09).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group II violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a six-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the First Group II violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors
(University Hospital police officers took custody of the female from the patrol car and walked
her into the hospital, no active discipline) outweigh aggravating factors (none).

Rationale: The member did not intervene when he allowed a female to be transported to
University Hospital while naked. Once at the hospital, he had a second opportunity for the
female to access a covering for her body, but again the member failed to intervene when he
allowed University Hospital police officers to walk the naked female into the hospital.

Specifications: Improper search (General Police Order 3.01.01) – Operated a Cleveland Police
vehicle that was involved a motor vehicle crash that was reviewed by the Traffic Safety Analysis
Panel (TSAP) and determined to be preventable (Manual of Rules 4.03, 6.03 and 6.05).

Disposition: The improper search charge more closely comports with a Group I Violation
(conduct that has a negative impact on the operations or professional image of the Division or
that negatively impacts relationships with other officers, agencies or the public); the charge was
amended from a Group II violation to a Group I violation. Found “Guilty” of two Group I
violations. Considering the member’s active discipline history (none) the member received a
Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction for the preventable motor vehicle accident and a two-
day suspension for the remaining charge. This corrective action is within the First Group I
violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (the existence of an injury risk
to officers or jail personnel) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline).

Rationale: The member was assisting in an arrest and search incident to arrest of a combative
male, the member and three other officers believed the search was completed and he placed the
arrested male into his patrol car for transport to MetroHealth. Upon the arrested male being
evaluated and released from MetroHealth, the member placed him back into his patrol car and
conveyed him to the County Jail garage where a sergeant interviewed him regarding the force
used to take him into custody. While interviewing the male, the sergeant saw a suspicious bulge
in the male’s sweatshirt and found a large pair of pointed scissors, which could easily be used as
a weapon. The member was assisting a superior officer with this arrest, and because several 
officers were simultaneously searching the male, it was unclear what areas were searched, and as
such, this behavior more closely comports with a Group I violation. While the member was the
driver of a Division vehicle, he was involved in an accident which the Traffic Safety Analysis
Panel found to be preventable. Multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may
escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range.

Specification: Improper search (General Police Order 3.01.01).

Disposition: The charge more closely comports with a Group I Violation (conduct that has a
negative impact on the operations or professional image of the Division or that negatively
impacts relationships with other officers, agencies or the public); the charge was amended from a
Group II violation to a Group I violation. Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering
the member’s active discipline history (none) the member received a two-day suspension. This
corrective action is within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (the existence of an injury risk to officers or jail personnel) outweigh
mitigating factors (no active discipline).

Specification: Traveled outside of his assigned district without supervisory permission (Manual

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active

within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (at a
location outside of assigned district without permission, erroneous Duty Report) outweigh
mitigating factors (no active discipline).

Rationale: While the member’s Duty Report indicates he was refueling his vehicle at East 103rd
Street and Saint Clair Avenue, his vehicle’s Automatic Vehicle Locator data reveals that he was
on Parkgate Avenue. This location is outside of the member’s assigned district and an area for

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active

when he was placed on restricted duty. The member sought access once all supervisors who were
aware of his restrictions were no longer assigned to the Second District.

Specifications: Refused mandatory overtime; First Quarter of 2025 (×4) (Bureau of
Communications and Property Control Policy and Procedure; Mandatory Overtime Refusal
1.3.07, Manual of Rules 10.01) – Refused mandatory overtime; Second Quarter of 2025 (×7)
(Bureau of Communications and Property Control Policy and Procedure; Mandatory Overtime
Refusal 1.3.07, Manual of Rules 10.01).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of two Group I violations. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a five-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the Bureau of Communication and Property Control matrix for seven to eight mandatory
refusals of overtime in a quarter when mitigating factors (at the low end of refusals for the matrix
section - seven) outweigh aggravating factors (multiple violations).

Rationale: After the first refusal in each quarter is removed, the member did have four net
refusals of mandatory overtime in First Quarter of 2025, and seven net refusals in the Second
Quarter of 2025. Multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate the
disciplinary action to the maximum group range.

Specifications: Traveled outside of the City of Cleveland without supervisory permission
(Manual of Rules 4.06 and 4.07) – Erroneous reporting of location (General Police Order
1.07.06) – Failed to conduct himself as a role model to a probationary patrol officer General
Police Order 1.1.24 – Failed to notify the Division of a change of address (Manual of Rules 2.12
and Divisional Notice 25-054) – Traveled outside of his assigned zone without supervisory
permission (Manual of Rules 4.06 and 4.07).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of two Group I violations. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a two-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors
(multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action
to the maximum group range) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline).

Rationale: The member reported to Cleveland Police Radio that he was going to the Fourth
District to pick up property; however, he instead left the City of Cleveland without permission
and traveled to Warrensville Heights and South Euclid, all while acting as a Field Training
Officer. It was determined that the member actually resides at an address in Warrensville Heights
but he had not updated his address via the Change of Address Form. Unrelated to the previous
investigation, he was not patrolling his area of assignment, Zone 3, and was instead in Zone 1
(Warehouse/Entertainment District) without permission.
Charges: Travel Violation,Failure to Supervise,Failure to Report/Notify

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Failed to respond to the scene of a potential abduction and notify and brief the
District Commander (General Police Order 4.1.14).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (11/7/2024: one-day suspension, 1/15/2025: two-day suspension) the member
received a two-day suspension. This corrective action is within the Group I violation range of the
discipline matrix when aggravating factors (dissimilar active Group I discipline) outweigh
mitigating factors (accepted responsibility).

Rationale: While the member did ultimately respond to the scene of the potential abduction, he
did not notify his commander or a superior officer of this significant event. While a Combined
Abduction Response Team (CART) call-up did not occur, had one been necessary, his failure
could have been catastrophic.

Specification: Improper search (General Police Order 3.01.01).

Disposition: The charge more closely comports with a Group I Violation (conduct that has a
negative impact on the operations or professional image of the Division or that negatively
impacts relationships with other officers, agencies or the public); the charge was amended from a
Group II violation to a Group I violation. Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering
the member’s active discipline history (2/3/2023: six-day suspension) the member received a
two-day suspension. This corrective action is within the First Group I violation range of the
discipline matrix when aggravating factors (the existence of an injury risk to officers or jail
personnel, active discipline) outweigh mitigating factors (none).

Rationale: The member was the first on scene and was assisting in the arrest, and search
incident to that arrest of a combative male. After he and three other officers believed the search
was completed, members of 1A13 placed the arrested male into their patrol car for transport.
Ultimately, the arrested male was conveyed to the County Jail garage where a sergeant
interviewed him regarding the force used to take him into custody. While interviewing the male,
the sergeant saw a suspicious bulge in the arrested male’s sweatshirt and found a large pair of
pointed scissors, which could easily be used as a weapon. The member was assisting a superior
officer with this arrest, and because several officers were simultaneously searching the male, it
was unclear what areas were searched. Further, the member was not the one that placed the
arrested male into a patrol car. Based on the foregoing, this behavior more closely comports with
a Group I violation.

Specification: Tardy (three times in 30-calendar day period) (Manual of Rules 10.01).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (5/19/2025: 13-day suspension) the member received a three-day suspension.
This corrective action is within the Second Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (similar active discipline) outweigh mitigating factors (none).
Rationale: Timeliness is critical in the Communications Control Section, as dispatchers need to
be properly relieved before they can report off. The member has repeatedly (September 4, 18,
and 23, 2025) failed to report to work on time, causing a delay in the relief of her fellow
dispatchers.

Specifications: Verbal threat (General Police Order 1.1.08) – Workplace Violence Policy
violation (General Police Order 1.1.08) – Failed to report a Level 2 Use of Force (General
Police Order 2.01.05).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation and two Group III violations. Considering
the member’s active discipline history (none) the member received a fifteen-day suspension.
This corrective action is within the First Group III Violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (multiple violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate the
disciplinary action to the maximum group range) outweigh mitigating factors (none).
Rationale: The member answered the phone at the Fifth District and made a verbal threat to
assault another member of the Cleveland Division of Police. The member attended a Fifth
District holiday party at Mulberry’s Bar when the member referred to another member of the
Cleveland Division of Police as a derogatory term, this member and their companion both
perceived this action as threatening, causing them to leave the premises. These actions are in
violation of the City of Cleveland’s Workplace Violence Policy and General Police Order 1.1.08.
In addition, the member failed to report a Level 2 Use of Force. Multiple violations arising from
a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range.
Charges: Violence in the Workplace,Failure to Report/Intervene (UOF),Unprofessional Conduct

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Accessed a law enforcement database (OHLEG) for a reason that was deemed
outside of a legitimate law enforcement or criminal justice purpose (General Police Order 1.1.39
and Manual of Rules 9.15).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group II violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a six-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the First Group II violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors
(accepted responsibility) outweigh aggravating factors (none).
Rationale: The member conducted a search in a law enforcement database (OHLEG) for
personal use which was not for a law enforcement or criminal justice purpose.

Specification: Ohio Law Enforcement Gateway (OHLEG) violation (General Police Order
1.1.39 and Manual of Rules 9.15).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group II violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a six-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the First Group II violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors
(accepted responsibility) outweigh aggravating factors (none).
Rationale: The member conducted a search in the Ohio Law Enforcement Gateway (OHLEG)
database that was not for a law enforcement or criminal justice purpose.

Specification: Ohio Law Enforcement Gateway (OHLEG) violation (General Police Order
1.1.39 and Manual of Rules 9.15).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group II violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a six-day suspension. This corrective action is

Rationale: The member conducted a search in the Ohio Law Enforcement Gateway (OHLEG)

Specification: Wearable Camera System (WCS) violation (General Police Order 4.06.04).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (11/21/2024: three-day suspension, 3/31/2025: one-day suspension) the
member received a two-day suspension. This corrective action is within the Second Group I
violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (accepted responsibility)
outweigh aggravating factors (active discipline).
Rationale: The member failed to activate his Wearable Camera System (WCS) prior to engaging
in a vehicle pursuit. During the hearing, he did accept responsibility and stated that when he
activated his emergency lights and sirens to position 3, it did not activate his WCS. Per
Divisional policy, it is still the members’ responsibility to ensure that his WCS is activated prior
to responding to all calls for service.

Specification: Emergency response driving violation (General Police Order 4.01.01).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (3/31/2025: Written Reprimand) the member received a one-day suspension.
This corrective action is within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when
aggravating factors (active discipline) outweigh mitigating factors (none).
Rationale: The member failed to maintain an audible siren during an emergency response.
While in emergency response, the member had emergency overhead lights activated but did not
have an siren audible while traveling through an intersection on a red light and continued to use
the siren intermittently while traveling toward the suspect vehicle’s location during a second
pursuit.
