Hearing Held By: Acting Deputy Chief of Field Operations
Charge & Discipline Decision:
Failed to provide guidance to officers on scene and ensure that an assault
report is created (General Police Order 2.2.02, Divisional Notice 20-104 and 20-110 and
Manual of Rules 1.06, 4.01, 4.18 and 4.19) – Failed to wear Personal Protective Equipment
(PPE) and failed to enforce that PPE is worn by all responding officers (Divisional Notice 20-
124 and 20-125). Even though the scene was chaotic, the officer should have ensured that a
police report was completed on-scene rather than sending the victim to a district headquarters
where the report was completed. The officer took responsibility for the violation (pled “No
Contest”). Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the officer’s active discipline
history (12/13/2021 – one-day suspension), the officer received a Written Reprimand. This
corrective action is within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix where
mitigating factors (circumstances under which the rule was violated (chaotic scene)) outweigh
aggravating factors (supervisory rank of officer and active discipline). The officer failed to
ensure compliance with Division Policy regarding N-95 procedures. Reinstruction is appropriate
in this instance. The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.
Charges: Failure to Supervise,Uniform/Grooming Violation,Failure to Report/Notify

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Failed to properly investigate the complainant’s Motor Vehicle Accident (MVA)
(General Police Order 8.1.03 and Manual of Rules 4.18) – Failed to place his Wearable Camera
System (WCS) in event mode during the entirety of an investigation (General Police Order
4.06.04).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the officer’s active discipline
history (none), the officer received a Written Reprimand for failing to properly investigate a
complainant’s MVA. This corrective action is within the First Group I violation range of the
discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no active discipline, accepted responsibility) outweigh
aggravating factors (none). In addition, the member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of
Reinstruction for failing to place his WCS into event mode.

Rationale: The officer failed to follow up with the victim of an accident after they were released
from the hospital and failed to note any witness information that was obtained at the scene of the
accident. The officer did fail to place his Wearable Camera System into event mode during the
entirety of the investigation. Because this is the officer’s first Wearable Camera System
violation, reinstruction is appropriate regarding this instance.

Specification: Negligently caused a revolver to discharge by handling a firearm unsafely while
attempting to secure it (General Police Order 5.08.01 and Manual of Rules 4.03).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the officer’s active discipline
history (none), the officer received a Written Reprimand. This corrective action is within the
First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no active
discipline, was unaware of the revolver’s presence) outweigh aggravating factors (possible
serious injury to a citizen). In addition, the officer shall attend retraining through the Training
Section on Firearm Handling.

Rationale: Negligently causing a revolver to discharge charge was amended from a Group II
violation to a Group I violation. The officer was attempting to clear a home of firearms and in an
attempt to render a “Tommy-Gun” style weapon safe, negligently caused an unseen revolver to
discharge in the presence of a citizen. The discharge occurred while he was reaching up onto a
shelf for the “Tommy-Gun” and something then struck the unseen revolver, causing it to
discharge.

Specification: Failed to report a Level 1 joint manipulation and Level 2 takedown (General
Police Order 2.01.05).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the officer’s active discipline
history (none), the officer received a Written Reprimand. This corrective action is within the
First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no active
discipline, force used was within policy) outweigh aggravating factors (failing to report the use
of force presents an appearance of impropriety). In addition, the officer shall attend retraining
through the Training Section on Use of Force Reporting.

Rationale: The officer did engage a suspect who physically resisted his attempts to peaceably
remove him from the Greyhound Bus Station. Due to this resistance, the officer used Level 2
force, which was within policy, to remove the suspect from the property. However, the officer
did fail to properly report this use of force to his supervisor when she arrived on scene.

Specification: Failed to report a Level 2 takedown (General Police Order 2.01.05).

Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the officer’s active discipline
history (none), the officer received a Written Reprimand. This corrective action is within the
First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no active
discipline, force used was within policy) outweigh aggravating factors (failing to report the use
of force presents an appearance of impropriety). In addition, the officer shall attend retraining
through the Training Section on Use of Force Reporting.

Rationale: The officer did engage a suspect who physically resisted his attempts to peaceably
remove him from the Greyhound Bus Station. Due to this resistance, the officer used Level 2
force, which was within policy, to remove the suspect from the property. However, the officer
did fail to properly report this use of force to his supervisor when she arrived on scene.

Hearing Held By: Deputy Chief of Homeland Special Operations
Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Walked to the front of a vehicle and stood in its path while the driver drove
toward him (General Police Orders 2.01.03).

Disposition: The charge of walking to the front of a vehicle and standing in its path more closely
comports with a Group I Violation (conduct that has a negative impact on the operations or
professional image of the Division or that negatively impacts relationships with other officers,
agencies or the public), the charge was amended from a Group II violation to a Group I violation.
Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the officer’s active discipline history
(none), the officer received a Written Reprimand. This corrective action is within the First Group
I violation range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no active discipline) outweigh
aggravating factors (none). In additional, the officer shall attend retraining at the Training
Section on tactics, specifically proper positioning with a suspect vehicle.

Rationale: The officer pled “No Contest” and accepted responsibility. The officer did place
himself in front of a vehicle and stood in its path while the driver drove slowly toward him.

Specifications: Failed to properly secure a prisoner by not handcuffing the prisoner which
allowed the prisoner to escape (General Police Order 3.01.01 and 3.02.02, and Manual of Rules
2.03 and 8.03) – Failed to activate his Wearable Camera System (General Police Order 4.06.04).

Disposition: The charge of failing to properly secure a prisoner more closely comports with a
Group I Violation (conduct that has a negative impact on the operations or professional image of
the Division or that negatively impacts relationships with other officers, agencies or the public),
the charge was amended from a Group II violation to a Group I violation. Found “Guilty” of two
Group I violations. The officer received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction for the WCS
charge. Considering the officer’s active discipline history (none), the officer received a Written
Reprimand for the remaining charge. This corrective action is within the First Group I violation
range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no active discipline, accepted
responsibility) outweigh aggravating factors (none).

Rationale: The officer accepted partial responsibility. The prisoner was handcuffed prior to
using the restroom, and removing the handcuffs was appropriate while the prisoner was in the
restroom, however, the officer did fail to properly secure a prisoner with handcuffs when he
exited the restroom. The officer did fail to keep his Wearable Camera System recording without
gaining a supervisor’s approval to place the Wearable Camera System into buffering mode. This
is the officer’s first Wearable Camera System violation; reinstruction is appropriate in this
instance.

Specifications: Wearable Camera System (WCS) violation (General Police Order 4.06.04) –
Operated a Cleveland Police vehicle that was involved in a motor vehicle crash that was
reviewed by the Traffic Safety Analysis Panel (TSAP) and determined to be preventable
(Manual of Rules 4.03, 6.03 and 6.05).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of two Group I violations. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a Written Reprimand for her second preventable
crash within two years. In addition, the member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of
Reinstruction for the remaining charge. This corrective action is within the Group I violation
range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no active discipline) outweigh
aggravating factors (second preventable crash within two years).
Rationale: The member operated a patrol car while on-duty that was involved in a motor vehicle
crash in which the Traffic Safety Analysis Panel (TSAP) determined to be preventable. While
the lights and siren were activated, she failed to proceed through an intersection with due regard
for the safety of all persons and property. The member failed to activate her Wearable Camera
System while responding to a call for service as required.

Hearing Held By: "Assistant Director of Public Safety, Administrative Operations"
Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Did knowingly acquire financial reimbursement of $937.50 from the City of
Cleveland via the Cleveland Division of Police Tuition Reimbursement Program while also
receiving financial reimbursement from the United States Air Force’s tuition assistance program
for $1,500. This appropriation of funds from the City is beyond the scope of expressed or
implied consent of the Division’s Tuition Reimbursement Program. This conduct would
constitute the crime of Petty Theft under City of Cleveland Codified Ordinance 625.05, a
Misdemeanor of the First Degree (General Police Order 1.3.21, and Manual of Rules 2.01 and
5.01).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of one Group I violation. Considering the officer’s active discipline
history (12/29/2020: fourteen-day suspension), the officer received a Written Reprimand. This
corrective action is within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when
mitigating factors (accepted responsibility, paid restitution to the City of Cleveland) outweigh
aggravating factors (active discipline).

Rationale: Because the officer’s intentions were not to defraud the system and flaws in the pre-
structured Form-1 associated with General Police Order 1.3.21 were clearly articulated by a

supervisory officer during the pre-disciplinary hearing, the Group III violation was amended to a
Group I violation for erroneous reporting.
